Considering cooperation performance among agents is essential.
On MATH dataset, we compare the average numbers of different viewpoint collisions (DVC) and correct viewpoint revision (CVR) per question of our CortexDebate with and without using intimacy (I) and self-orientation (S) factors. As shown in Table
3
, we can find that incorporating I and S increases both the frequency and quality of viewpoint interactions. This confirms that considering interactions among agents is essential for enhancing collective reasoning in LLM-based systems.
CortexDebate excels in large-scale debates.
To explore the influence of LLM agent number and debating rounds on our CortexDebate, we evaluate the task performance of CortexDebate under different numbers of participating LLM agents and debating rounds. We present the results in Figure
5
. We can see that as the number of LLM agents and the debating rounds increase, the task performance of our CortexDebate continues to improve. Moreover, compared with debating rounds, the increase in the number of LLM agents contributes more to the performance improvement of CortexDebate. These results demonstrate the potential of CortexDebate for application in large-scale debates.
CortexDebate retains helpful debates.
To further show the effectiveness of the retained edges and corresponding nodes in our CortexDebate, we conduct a detailed analysis. On MATH dataset, we calculate the average debating number (Avg-DN) and RA of every participated LLM agent under the fully-connected graph (FcG) and our proposed debating graph. As shown in Table
4
, our proposed CortexDebate reduces debates among the LLM agents, while improving the performance of every LLM agent. It suggests that our CortexDebate retains helpful debates while pruning harmful ones.
6
Conclusion
In this paper, we propose a new MAD method termed “CortexDebate” to improve the reasoning abilities of multi-agent interaction systems. Specifically, our CortexDebate establishes a sparse debating graph among participating LLM agents, which reduces input information burdens of LLM agents. Besides, by integrating the McKinsey Trust Formula, our proposed MDM module conducts credible evaluations to gradually optimize the debating graph, making the debating process equal, in-depth, and effective. Due to the above designs, our method alleviates two major issues faced by existing MAD systems (
i.e.
, too lengthy input contexts and overconfidence-caused unequal debates), and shows superior performance to various state-of-the-art MAD methods on various typical tasks. In the future, we plan to continue exploring the potential of CortexDebate in large-scale debates and complex tasks (
i.e.
, domain expert systems).
Limitations
Despite the impressive performance of our proposed CortexDebate, we acknowledge that it has two main limitations. Firstly, as a multi-agent debate method, compared with single-agent methods, it is inevitable that there will be a decrease in efficiency and an increase in cost when solving tasks. Secondly, despite the success, the reasoning ability of LLM agents remains an important factor that limits the performance of CortexDebate. Although our proposed CortexDebate improves the debate strategy among LLM agents, mistakes may still occur due to the poor reasoning ability of LLM agents.

Appendix A
Dataset Details
The eight datasets used in our experiments are classic datasets that are widely employed to evaluate the performance of agent-based methods. Here, we provide an introduction to the eight datasets used in our experiments.
GSM-IC.
It is a grade-school math problem dataset derived from GSM8K
(Cobbe et al.,
2021
)
. For each problem in GSM8K, GSM-IC keeps the base problem description and adds to it one irrelevant sentence that does not affect the solution of the problem.
MATH.
It is a math dataset containing challenging competition mathematics problems. Each of them has a full step-by-step solution.
MMLU.
It contains 57 types of multiple-choice problems, such as elementary mathematics, US history, computer science, and so on. To acquire high performance on the MMLU datasset, models must possess extensive world knowledge and strong problem-solving ability.
MMLU-pro.
It contains questions sourced from multiple origins, such as MMLU, TheoremQA, and SciBench. Moreover, it expands the option number of each problem from 4 to 10.
GPQA.
It contains 448 graduate-level question-answering problems, covering knowledge in various fields such as biology, physics, and chemistry.
ARC-C.
It contains complex questions on natural science, presented in the form of multiple-choice options.

LongBench.
It is a dataset designed to evaluate the long-context understanding capabilities of models. It encompasses six major categories of tasks, including single-document QA, multi-document QA, summarization, few-shot learning, code completion, and synthetic tasks.
SQuAD.
It is a dataset used to evaluate the reading comprehension ability of models. The dataset requires models to answer different questions from given long texts.

Appendix C
Additional Experiments
In this section, we present large-scale experiments of our CortexDebate and baseline methods to further demonstrate the superiority of CortexDebate.
Experimental Setup.
For each task (
i.e.
, math, world knowledge question answering, reasoning, and long-context understanding), we conduct experiments on the more challenging one of the two datasets (
i.e.
, MATH, MMLU-pro, GPQA, and LongBench). For each adopted dataset, we experiment on a subset of 1000 examples. Besides, the backbone models and evaluation metrics used in the experiments are the same as mentioned in Section
5.1
.
Results.
The experimental results are presented in Table
5
. Consistent with the experimental results reported in Table
1
, our proposed CortexDebate achieves the best performance on all the datasets compared with baseline methods. For instance, CortexDebate achieves a maximal RA of 56.30% on MATH dataset, 58.90% on MMLU-pro dataset, 36.60% on GPQA dataset, and 59.63% on LongBench dataset, respectively. Moreover, for each method, we calculate the average token numbers of the contexts input to one LLM agent on each dataset and present the results in Table
6
. Compared with the full debate methods (
i.e.
, MLD, RECONCILE, ChatEval, and MPRC), our CortexDebate significantly reduces the length of the contextual input for each LLM agent, with a maximum reduction of 65.50%. Moreover, compared with the part debate methods (
i.e.
, GD and ND), our CortexDebate can reduce the input context length by at least 17.40%.

In this section, we present additional in-depth investigations on our CortexDebate to further analyze its effectiveness.
D.1
Average-based Edge Pruning
As present in Equation (
11
), our CortexDebate uses the average value as the threshold to prune the edges. To evaluate the effectiveness of adopting the average-based threshold, we conduct experiments on MATH dataset. Specifically, we compare the strategy of debating with the agents above the average threshold (AAT) with three alternatives, namely Top-3 (debating with the top three agents), Bot-3 (excluding the bottom three agents), and AMT (debating with agents above the median threshold). As shown in Table
9
, AAT achieves superior performance than the others, which validates the effectiveness of the average-based edge pruning.
D.2
Text Similarity Calculation
In the experiments, we use the text-embedding-3-large model released by OpenAI as the embedding model. For calculation of text similarity, we use cosine similarity which is widely used due to its efficiency and robustness to varying input length, and strong empirical performance. To confirm this, on LongBench dataset, we compare our strategy with ag-nli-DeTS-sentence-similarity-v4 model (DeTS) that is trained on six natural language inference datasets and “DeepSeek R1 + prompt” (R1-P). The average task scores and variances over three runs are reported in Table
10
. We can find that our method outperforms the two baseline methods. Moreover, we observe that large language models tend to perform unstably when used for text similarity estimation, which is reflected by the higher variance across different runs.
D.3
Confidence Recalibration
As present in Equation (
2
), our CortexDebate adopts a recalibration strategy to mitigate overconfidence issue of LLM agents. Following the practice of
Chen et al. (
2023
)
, we conduct experiments on different combinations of thresholds. We present the average scores on eight adopted datasets in Table
11
. According to the results, this recalibration strategy leads to the improved performance, and the parameter setting in our paper leads to the best performance. Moreover, our method is generally insensitive to different threshold configurations. This demonstrates the effectiveness and robustness of the proposed confidence recalibration strategy.

Appendix E
Introduction of Evaluation Strategies
Here we introduce the details of the evaluation strategies (
i.e.
, self-evaluation, peer evaluation, part MDM) mentioned in Section
5.3
.
Sel

toolCalls.name: Bash

input.description: Read remaining chars of paper

toolCalls.result:
f-evaluation.
In this strategy, each LLM agent is required to generate a confidence score for its generated answer. Each LLM agent will only debate with the LLM agents whose confidence scores are above the average of the entire graph.
Peer Evaluation.
For each LLM agent, its answer is scored by other LLM agents, and the final score of the answer is the average of the received scores. Each LLM agent will only debate with the LLM agents whose scores are above the average of the entire graph.
MDM (w/o

)).
For McKinsey Trust Formula used in MDM module, this strategy only considers the first two aspects (
i.e.
, credibility and reliability) which evaluate individual abilities, neglecting the last two aspects (
i.e.
, intimacy and self-orientation) which evaluate the debate effectiveness between two LLM agents.
Appendix F
Prompts in CortexDebate
Type
Prompt
Initial Answer

Please think it step by step and generate an answer and an explanation for your answer.
Also, evaluate how confident you are that your answer is correct. Your confidence score should between 0 and 1.

Table 12:
Prompts of our proposed CortexDebate used in the experiments.
We provide the specific prompts of our proposed CortexDebate in Table
12
. For initial answer generation, CortexDebate follows
Kojima et al. (
2022
)
and prompts each LLM agent to solve the problem step by step. For answer regeneration, the prompt contains three parts: (a) An instruction that stimulates LLM agents to generate their new answers and self-confidence scores after scrutinizing other answers. (b) A description of the problem. (c) Some answers generated by other LLM agents.
Appendix G
Introduction of Baseline Methods
Here we introduce the details of the baseline methods (
i.e.
, Multi-agent Voting, ChatEval, Multi-LLM Debate, RECONCILE, Peer Review Debate, GroupDebate, and Neighbor Debate) in our experiments.
Multi-agent Voting.
This method adopts a majority voting strategy to aggregate responses from multiple LLM agents. Specifically, each LLM agent independently generates a response to the given question. The final prediction is then determined through majority voting.
ChatEval.
ChatEval uses an extra LLM agent to summarize the debating results in each round of debate. The specific prompt of debating summary used in the experiments is shown in Figure
7
. The summary text generated in the current round of debate will be input to each LLM agent as supplementary information in the next round of debate.
Multi-LLM Debate.
Firstly, each LLM generates an answer to the question. Then, each LLM reads and critiques the answers generated by other LLM agents, and generates its new answer. This step is repeated multiple times. After that, the final answer is obtained through majority voting among the answers generated by all the LLM agents in the last round of debate. The specific prompt used in the experiments is shown in Figure
6
.
Figure 6:
Prompt of Multi-LLM Debate used in the experiments.
Figure 7:
Debating summary prompt of ChatEval used in the experiments.
Figure 8:
Prompt of RECONCILE used in the experiments.
RECONCILE.
Given a problem, each LLM first generates an answer and its uncertainty for the answer. Then
all LLM agents enter a multi-round debate. Each debating round consists of each LLM generating a revised answer and its new uncertainty based on the answers generated by all other LLM agents from the previous round. After the multi-round debate, RECONCILE obtains the final answer through majority voting. The specific prompt used in the experiments is shown in Figure
8
.
Figure 9:
Prompt of Peer Review Debate used in the experiments.
Figure 10:
Prompt of GroupDebate Debate used in the experiments.
Figure 11:
Prompt of Neighbor Debate used in the experiments.
Peer Review Debate.
Similar to RECONCILE, this method also evaluates all the answers in each round of debate. However, this method employs a peer review strategy where the answer generated by each LLM agent is evaluated by other LLM agents. The specific prompt used in the experiments is shown in Figure
9
.
GroupDebate.
This method divides the LLM agents into several debate groups, with each group conducting internal debates. After the internal debates, the result of each debate group is summarized and placed into a shared pool. After that, each group retrieves the debate summaries of all groups from the pool, which serve as the input for all the LLM agents in the next round. The specific prompt used in the experiments is shown in Figure
10
.
Neighbor Debate.
In this method, LLMs only debate with their neighbors. The specific prompt used in the experiments is shown in Figure
11
.

# CortexDebate: Debating Sparsely and Equally for Multi-Agent Debate

> 受人脑白质启发，通过稀疏辩论图和 McKinsey Trust Formula 解决多智能体辩论中的上下文过长和过度自信两大问题。

## 论文元信息

| 字段 | 内容 |
|------|------|
| **标题** | CortexDebate: Debating Sparsely and Equally for Multi-Agent Debate |
| **作者** | Yiliu Sun, Zicheng Zhao, Sheng Wan*, Chen Gong* |
| **机构** | 南京理工大学计算机科学与工程学院；南京农业大学人工智能学院；上海交通大学自动化与智能感知学院 |
| **会议** | ACL 2025 Findings |
| **发表时间** | 2025 年 |
| **arXiv ID** | 2507.03928 |
| **代码链接** | 论文未提供 |
| **引用数** | 4（截至 2026-05） |

## 核心问题

现有 Multi-Agent Debate (MAD) 方法面临两大关键问题：

1. **输入上下文过长**：每个 LLM 智能体需要与所有其他智能体辩论，随着智能体数量和辩论轮数增加，输入上下文急剧膨胀，导致 LLM 在大量输入信息中"迷失"，性能下降。
2. **过度自信困境 (Overconfidence Dilemma)**：先前方法仅根据智能体自身的置信度决定辩论影响力，过度自信的智能体逐渐主导整个辩论过程，其他"弱"智能体提供的潜在有用信息被忽略，导致辩论不平等、效果低下。

## 核心方法

### 整体思路

受人脑认知理论启发——人脑在面对问题时，不同皮层区域之间会建立动态、稀疏的网络，这个网络由白质 (white matter) 逐步优化。CortexDebate 将 LLM 智能体视作皮层区域，构建稀疏有向辩论图，每个智能体只与对自己有帮助的智能体辩论。

### 三阶段框架

1. **Phase 1: 初始答案生成** — 每个智能体独立生成答案、解释和置信度分数，并对置信度进行重校准 (Recalibration) 以缓解过度自信
2. **Phase 2: 多轮辩论** — 包含四个步骤：
   - **Step 1: 边权重优化** — 通过 MDM 模块计算辩论图中每条边的权重
   - **Step 2: 稀疏图建立** — 移除权重低于平均值的边，构建稀疏辩论图
   - **Step 3: 答案再生成** — 智能体根据稀疏图中相连的对手答案重新生成自己的答案
   - **Step 4: 辩论终止** — 检查是否达成共识或达到最大轮数
3. **Phase 3: 最终答案生成** — 通过多数投票获得最终答案

### McKinsey Trust Formula 与 MDM 模块

McKinsey Trust Formula 原始形式：

$$T = \frac{C \times R \times I}{S}$$

MDM 模块将其四个因素适配到 MAD 场景：

| 因素 | 原始含义 | MAD 适配 | 计算方式 |
|------|---------|----------|---------|
| **Credibility (C)** | 专业能力 | 智能体的专业能力 | 通过 Scaling Law 计算预训练损失的倒数 |
| **Reliability (R)** | 任务表现稳定性 | 历史辩论中置信度分数的均值 | 递推平均：$R_d = \frac{R_{d-1} \times (d-1) + H_i^{d-1}}{d}$ |
| **Intimacy (I)** | 与被评估者的关系 | 两智能体观点差异程度（观点碰撞增强辩论效果） | $I_d = 1 - \overline{Sim}_d$（1 减去余弦相似度的历史均值） |
| **Self-Orientation (S)** | 自我导向程度 | 智能体参与辩论的频率（参与越少越自私） | $S_d = (d-1) \times (n-1) - P_d$ |

边权重计算公式：

$$W_{i \to j}^d = \frac{C_d \times R_d \times I_d}{S_d}$$

> [!tip] 理解提示（非原文）
> 公式的直觉含义：一个好的辩论对手应该(1)自身能力强 (C)，(2)表现稳定可靠 (R)，(3)与你的观点有差异能产生碰撞 (I)，(4)积极参与群体讨论而非自我封闭 (S 作为分母，参与越多 S 越小，权重越大)。这比单纯看置信度更全面，有效缓解了过度自信问题。

### 稀疏图剪枝策略

对每个智能体 $A_j$，计算指向它的所有边的平均权重 $\overline{W}_j^d$，移除权重低于平均值的边：

$$W_{i \to j}^d = \begin{cases} 1, & W_{i \to j} \geq \overline{W}_j^d \\ 0, & W_{i \to j} < \overline{W}_j^d \end{cases}$$

## 实验设置

| 维度 | 内容 |
|------|------|
| **数据集** | 数学：GSM-IC, MATH；世界知识 QA：MMLU, MMLU-pro；推理：GPQA, ARC-C；长上下文理解：LongBench, SQuAD |
| **基线方法** | 无辩论：MaV；全辩论：MLD, RECONCILE, ChatEval, PRD；部分辩论：GD, ND |
| **指标** | RA (Result Accuracy), M-Avg (Macro-Average for LongBench), EM (Exact Match for SQuAD) |
| **骨干模型** | Qwen-2.5-7B-Instruct-Turbo, Mistral-7B-Instruct, Typhoon-1.5-8B-Instruct, Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct-Turbo, Gemma-2-9B-Instruct |
| **实验规模** | 每数据集 100 个样本，每实验 3 次运行取平均 |
| **最大辩论轮数** | 5 轮 |

## 主要实验结果

### 主实验对比（8 数据集，100 样本）

| 类型 | 方法 | GSM-IC | MATH | MMLU | MMLU-pro | GPQA | ARC-C | LongBench | SQuAD |
|------|------|--------|------|------|----------|------|-------|-----------|-------|
| 无辩论 | MaV | 70.33 | 46.00 | 69.33 | 46.00 | 27.33 | 76.00 | 45.11 | 85.33 |
| 全辩论 | MLD | 72.67 | 47.33 | 71.33 | 47.33 | 28.33 | 79.33 | 48.87 | 86.33 |
| 全辩论 | RECONCILE | 75.67 | 50.33 | 75.00 | 53.67 | 31.00 | 83.67 | 52.55 | 88.33 |
| 全辩论 | ChatEval | 74.33 | 49.00 | 73.00 | 49.33 | 31.33 | 82.67 | 53.56 | 87.33 |
| 全辩论 | PRD | 77.00 | 51.33 | 77.33 | 54.00 | 32.00 | 84.33 | 50.21 | 87.67 |
| 部分辩论 | GD | 76.00 | 49.67 | 74.00 | 51.67 | 32.67 | 82.00 | 55.97 | 90.33 |
| 部分辩论 | ND | 73.67 | 49.00 | 71.67 | 48.67 | 32.33 | 81.33 | 54.55 | 88.33 |
| **本文** | **CortexDebate** | **79.33** | **56.00** | **82.33** | **59.33** | **36.33** | **88.33** | **60.31** | **93.33** |

### 关键发现

- **输入上下文长度缩减**：相比全辩论方法，CortexDebate 最大缩减 **70.79%** 的输入上下文长度
- **平均得分最高**：CortexDebate 在 8 数据集上平均得分 **69.41%**，次优方法仅 66.71%（PRD）
- **消融实验**：稀疏图 + MDM 缺一不可，移除 Intimacy 和 Self-Orientation 因子后性能从 69.41% 降至 66.69%

### 大规模实验（1000 样本）

| 方法 | MATH | MMLU-pro | GPQA | LongBench |
|------|------|----------|------|-----------|
| MaV | 47.40 | 46.30 | 29.10 | 43.35 |
| PRD | 51.20 | 54.20 | 32.40 | 47.67 |
| **CortexDebate** | **56.30** | **58.90** | **36.60** | **59.63** |

## 论文不足与可改进之处

1. **效率与成本问题**：作为多智能体辩论方法，相比单智能体方法必然存在效率下降和成本增加。论文未提供具体的时间和 API 调用成本分析。

2. **受限于底层 LLM 的推理能力**：CortexDebate 改进了辩论策略，但如果底层 LLM 推理能力不足，错误仍会发生。方法本身无法突破模型能力的上限。

3. **稀疏图阈值策略较简单**：仅使用平均值作为剪枝阈值（AAT），缺乏自适应或学习型阈值机制。论文虽然在附录中对比了 Top-3、Bot-3、AMT 等替代策略，但都属启发式方法。

4. **Credibility 计算依赖 Scaling Law 的假设**：Credibility 分数直接使用 Chinchilla Scaling Law 的预训练损失来衡量模型能力，这假设模型的实际任务表现与其预训练损失严格相关，但在特定任务上可能不准确（如一个预训练损失低但在推理任务上表现差的模型）。

5. **实验规模有限**：主实验仅在每个数据集 100 个样本上运行，虽然附录提供了 1000 样本的大规模实验，但整体实验仅使用 7B-9B 参数量的开源模型，未在更大参数模型（如 GPT-4、Claude）上验证。

6. **缺乏与更多稀疏通信拓扑方法的对比**：论文引用了 Google 的 "Improving Multi-Agent Debate with Sparse Communication Topology" 工作，但未将其作为基线进行对比。

7. **Self-Orientation 的间接度量**：用辩论参与次数间接反映自我导向程度，这一映射关系建立在"参与越少越自私"的假设上，可能在某些场景下不完全成立。

# CortexDebate — 引言 (Section 1: Introduction)

> 本篇翻译论文第 1 节 Introduction 的内容。

## 正文翻译

### 背景：LLM 的局限与多智能体交互的兴起

近年来，受人类合作启发，许多多智能体交互方法 (multi-agent interaction methods) (Wan et al., 2024; Xu et al., 2023a; Tu et al., 2023; Hu et al., 2024) 被提出来进一步提升 LLM 的推理结果。这些方法旨在解决单一 LLM 智能体面临的关键问题，如幻觉 (hallucination) 和较差的推理能力。其中，Multi-Agent Debate (MAD) (Zhang et al., 2024a; Du et al., 2023) 已成为最有前景的策略之一，因为它可以通过智能体之间的辩论过程有效提升 LLM 智能体的性能。

### 现有 MAD 方法的两大缺陷

尽管先前的 MAD 方法取得了令人瞩目的成果，但它们仍然存在两大缺陷。如图 1 所示：

**缺陷 (a)：输入上下文过长。** 首先，在这些方法中，每个 LLM 智能体通常需要与所有其他 LLM 智能体辩论，这导致其输入上下文随着智能体数量和辩论轮数的增加而急剧膨胀。因此，由于单一 LLM 智能体通常难以处理过长的输入上下文 (Liu et al., 2024a; Luo et al., 2024)，它可能在大量输入信息中迷失，导致显著的性能下降。

**缺陷 (b)：过度自信困境 (Overconfidence Dilemma)。** 其次，先前的 MAD 方法仅根据每个智能体自身的置信度 (confidence) 来决定其辩论影响力，这可能导致过度自信的 LLM 智能体逐渐主导整个辩论过程。结果是，其他"弱"LLM 智能体提供的潜在有用信息可能被忽略。这种不平等的辩论对辩论效果是有害的，这一点也被 Xiong et al. (2023) 和 Xu et al. (2023b) 所证实。

> [!tip] 理解提示（非原文）
> 这两个问题本质上都是"全连接辩论图"结构的问题：(a) 是信息过载——连接太多导致每个节点接收的输入太长；(b) 是话语权不平等——简单按自信程度分配影响力，导致"嗓门大的说了算"。CortexDebate 的核心创新就是通过"稀疏图"解决 (a)，通过 McKinsey Trust Formula 解决 (b)。

### CortexDebate 的灵感来源与核心思路

因此，受人脑认知理论 (Thiebaut de Schotten and Forkel, 2022) 的启发，本文提出了一种新的 MAD 方法，名为 CortexDebate，它模仿了人脑皮层的工作模式。正如 Thiebaut de Schotten and Forkel (2022) 所揭示的，面对一个问题时，人脑倾向于在不同皮层区域之间建立一个动态且稀疏的网络，这个网络由一个专门的模块——白质 (white matter)——逐步优化。在优化过程中，白质更关注成对区域之间的影响力，而非单个皮层区域的性能。

将 LLM 智能体视作人脑中的皮层区域，CortexDebate 建立了一个稀疏的有向辩论图 (sparse directed debating graph)，其中节点代表参与的 LLM 智能体，边承载着两个智能体之间的信息传递。每条有向边被分配一个权重，反映通过辩论头节点智能体，尾节点智能体的性能预期提升程度。因此，每个尾节点智能体不会与那些不能帮助提升其性能的头节点智能体辩论。这意味着辩论图中权重较小的边将被移除，从而产生一个稀疏图。结果，输入到该尾节点智能体的上下文长度也将被缩减。

### MDM 模块：人工白质

为了优化辩论图的边权重，类比于人脑中白质动态调控不同皮层区域之间稀疏图的优化，CortexDebate 引入了一个名为 McKinsey-based Debate Matter (MDM) 的模块，作为人工白质。为缓解先前工作中存在的过度自信困境，MDM 在决定每条边权重时，同时考虑头节点智能体的性能和尾节点智能体的性能提升预期。具体来说，MDM 创新性地引入了 McKinsey Trust Formula (Lamarre et al., 2012) 来计算边权重，该公式在社会学中被广泛用于通过四个方面——可信度 (credibility)、可靠性 (reliability)、亲密性 (intimacy) 和自我导向 (self-orientation)——来评估一个人在群体中的可信度水平。其中，前两者评估个人能力，后两者评估与他人的协作效果。因此，该公式可以抑制过度自信的 LLM 智能体，并在 MAD 中平衡智能体的个人能力和团队协作能力。

> [!note] 译者注（非原文）
> "白质" (white matter) 是人脑中由有髓鞘神经纤维组成的结构，负责在不同脑区之间传递信号。论文将其类比为一个"网络路由优化器"——它决定哪些脑区之间应该建立更强的连接，哪些连接可以被忽略。MDM 模块正是这种"路由优化器"的人工实现。

### 实验效果概要

CortexDebate 的有效性已在多种任务上得到了充分验证，包括数学、世界知识问答、推理和长上下文理解。例如，与最先进的方法相比：

- 在数学任务上，CortexDebate 在 GSM-IC 数据集上将 Result Accuracy (RA) 提升了最高 **9.00%**，在 MATH 数据集上提升了 **10.00%**
- 在推理任务上，CortexDebate 在 GPQA 数据集上将 RA 提升了最高 **9.00%**，在 ARC-C 数据集上提升了 **12.33%**
- 此外，除了实现高性能外，CortexDebate 显著缩减了每个 LLM 智能体的输入上下文长度，最大缩减幅度达 **70.79%**

### 主要贡献

本文的主要贡献总结如下：

1. **提出 CortexDebate**：一种新的 MAD 方法，通过建立稀疏且动态的辩论图来提升 LLM 智能体的性能，减轻辩论过程中冗长输入上下文的负担。

2. **提出 MDM 模块**：引入 McKinsey Trust Formula 来同时评估每个 LLM 智能体的置信度和对其辩论组件的有用性，从而缓解 LLM 智能体的过度自信问题。

3. **广泛的实验验证**：在数学、世界知识问答、推理和长上下文理解等多种任务上进行了大量实验，证明 CortexDebate 优于代表性基线方法。

## 关键要点回顾

- 现有 MAD 方法存在两大缺陷：上下文过长和过度自信困境
- CortexDebate 受人脑白质优化皮层间连接的机制启发
- 核心创新是稀疏辩论图 + McKinsey Trust Formula 驱动的 MDM 模块
- 实验表明在多个任务上显著优于基线，同时大幅减少输入长度

## 下一步学习

# CortexDebate — 相关工作 (Section 2: Related Work)

> 本篇翻译论文第 2 节 Related Work 的内容。

## 正文翻译

在 MAD 系统中，每个 LLM 智能体提出自己的观点，并在多轮辩论中审视其他 LLM 智能体的观点 (Sun et al., 2024a)。总结来说，现有的 MAD 方法可以分为两类，即**顺序辩论 (Sequential Debate)** 和**并行辩论 (Parallel Debate)**。

### 顺序辩论 (Sequential Debate)

在这些方法中 (Hu et al., 2025; Brown-Cohen et al., 2023; Michael et al., 2023; Wang et al., 2025; He et al., 2024)，LLM 智能体按顺序生成观点。每个 LLM 智能体只能获取其前面的 LLM 智能体的观点。

例如，Liang et al. (2023) 要求两个 LLM 轮流反驳对方。除了辩手之外，Guan et al. (2025) 还添加了额外角色，如法官和评论家。法官在辩手之前发言解释任务，评论家最后发言总结辩论。

然而，在顺序辩论系统中，每个 LLM 智能体必须等待前面的智能体完成推理后才能开始。这导致辩论时间随 LLM 智能体数量线性增长，造成低效率，限制了可扩展性。

> [!tip] 理解提示（非原文）
> 顺序辩论就像"轮流发言"——A 说完 B 说，B 说完 C 说。虽然信息传递清晰（每个人都能看到前面所有人的发言），但效率很低：5 个智能体需要 5 倍的串行时间。这也是为什么 CortexDebate 选择并行辩论框架。

### 并行辩论 (Parallel Debate)

在这些方法中 (Pham et al., 2023; Yin et al., 2023; Chern et al., 2024; Khan et al., 2024; Liang et al., 2024; Li et al., 2024a; Hegazy, 2024; Zhang et al., 2024b)，所有 LLM 智能体基于上一辩论轮次中其他智能体的观点，同时生成自己的观点。

例如，Chan et al. (2023) 要求 LLM 智能体批判上一辩论轮次中生成的所有答案，并在每一轮同时更新自己的答案。除了上一轮生成的答案外，Sun et al. (2024b) 还为每个 LLM 智能体提供从网络检索到的与任务相关的信息。此外，一些方法 (Duan and Wang, 2024; Yoffe et al., 2024) 尝试调整每个 LLM 智能体的辩论影响力以提高辩论效果。例如，Chen et al. (2023) 要求每个 LLM 智能体为自己的答案生成置信度分数，然后将该分数与答案一起输入给其他 LLM 智能体。

### CortexDebate 与现有方法的区别

由于顺序辩论系统面临上述低效率问题，我们的 CortexDebate 遵循并行辩论框架。与要求每个 LLM 智能体在每轮中与所有其他智能体辩论的现有并行辩论方法相比，CortexDebate 通过在所有参与的 LLM 智能体之间建立**稀疏辩论图**来动态决定必要的辩论对手，从而缩短每个智能体的输入上下文。

这也有别于 Liu et al. (2024b) 和 Li et al. (2024b) 中的方法，后者的辩论对手是**固定的**。此外，与仅根据自身置信度确定每个 LLM 智能体辩论影响力的先前方法不同，我们引入了 McKinsey Trust Formula，使得每个 LLM 智能体的置信度和对其辩论组件的有用性都能得到评估。

> [!note] 译者注（非原文）
> 这里有一个重要的三分法对比：
> - **全辩论**（MLD, RECONCILE, ChatEval, PRD）：每个智能体与所有其他智能体辩论 → 连接太密集，上下文过长
> - **固定部分辩论**（GD, ND）：辩论对手是预先固定的 → 无法根据实际表现动态调整
> - **动态稀疏辩论**（CortexDebate）：通过 MDM 模块动态评估并选择最有价值的辩论对手 → 兼顾效率与效果

### 现有 MAD 方法分类汇总

| 类别 | 方法 | 特点 | 局限 |
|------|------|------|------|
| 顺序辩论 | Liang et al. (2023) | 两个 LLM 轮流反驳 | 时间随智能体数线性增长 |
| 顺序辩论 | Guan et al. (2025) | 添加法官和评论家角色 | 同上 |
| 并行辩论 | Chan et al. (2023) ChatEval | 每轮同时批判所有答案并更新 | 全连接 → 上下文过长 |
| 并行辩论 | Chen et al. (2023) RECONCILE | 生成置信度分数辅助辩论 | 仅依赖自身置信度 → 过度自信 |
| 并行辩论 | Liu et al. (2024b) GD | 分组辩论 | 对手固定，不能动态调整 |
| 并行辩论 | Li et al. (2024b) ND | 仅与邻居辩论 | 拓扑固定，缺乏优化 |
| **本文** | **CortexDebate** | **动态稀疏辩论图 + McKinsey Trust Formula** | **—** |

## 关键要点回顾

- MAD 方法分为顺序辩论和并行辩论两类
- 顺序辩论效率低，因此 CortexDebate 采用并行辩论框架
- 现有并行辩论方法要么全连接（上下文过长），要么固定拓扑（缺乏优化）
- CortexDebate 的核心优势：动态稀疏 + 可信度评估

## 下一步学习

- [[raw/lessons/CortexDebate/03-method|03 - 稀疏辩论图方法]]

# CortexDebate — 稀疏辩论图方法 (Section 3 & 4)

> 本篇翻译论文第 3 节 Preliminaries 和第 4 节 Methodology 的核心内容，包括问题定义、整体框架和稀疏辩论图的构建方法。

## 正文翻译

### 3.1 问题定义

CortexDebate 在 n 个 LLM 智能体之间建立一个有向辩论图 $\mathcal{G} = (\mathcal{A}, \mathcal{E})$，其中：

- **$\mathcal{A} = \{A_i\}_{i=1}^n$** 是顶点集，代表参与的 LLM 智能体
- **$\mathcal{E} = \{E_{i \to j}\}_{i,j \in [1,2,...,n]}$** 是有向边集，代表信息传输

每条有向边 $E_{i \to j}$ 被分配一个权重 $W_{i \to j}$，表示通过辩论智能体 $A_i$，智能体 $A_j$ 的性能预期提升程度。所有权重 $\{W_{i \to j}\}$ 在辩论过程中动态优化。

给定一个问题 $Q$，智能体 $\{A_i\}_{i=1}^n$ 进行 D 轮辩论。在第 d 轮辩论中，每个 LLM 智能体 $A_i$ 审视与其相连的 LLM 智能体的输出，然后生成自己的输出 $O_i^d$ 以及自置信度分数 $H_i^d$。之后，该辩论轮次的最终答案 $F_d$ 通过多数投票获得。

> [!tip] 理解提示（非原文）
> 这里的关键区分是"有向边"——$E_{i \to j}$ 表示 $A_i$ 对 $A_j$ 有帮助，但反过来 $A_j$ 对 $A_i$ 不一定有帮助。所以辩论图是有向的，边的权重也是不对称的。这意味着 $A_i$ 可能需要辩论 $A_j$ 的观点，但 $A_j$ 不一定需要辩论 $A_i$ 的观点。

### 4.1 Phase 1: 初始答案生成

给定问题 $Q$，CortexDebate 让每个 LLM 智能体 $A_i$ 独立生成初始输出 $O_i^0$ 和自置信度分数 $H_i^0$。为缓解过度自信，CortexDebate 采用重校准 (recalibration) 策略，该策略在先前工作中已被证明有效 (Chen et al., 2023)：

$$H_i^0 = \begin{cases} 0.8, & H_i^0 \geq 0.8 \\ 0.6, & 0.6 \leq H_i^0 < 0.8 \\ H_i^0, & 0.3 \leq H_i^0 < 0.6 \\ 0.3, & H_i^0 < 0.3 \end{cases}$$

> [!note] 译者注（非原文）
> 这个重校准策略的本质是"压缩置信度的高位和低位"：超过 0.8 的全部压到 0.8，低于 0.3 的全部抬到 0.3，中间区间的保持不变。这样防止某个智能体给出 0.99 的极端高置信度来主导辩论。

### 4.2 Phase 2: 多轮辩论

CortexDebate 进入辩论阶段，智能体集合 $\{A_i\}$ 进行 D 轮辩论。在第 d 轮辩论中，CortexDebate 包含四个步骤。

#### Step 1: 边权重优化

MDM 基于 Credibility、Reliability、Intimacy 和 Self-orientation 四个方面计算边权重。每个方面的具体计算如下：

**Credibility ($C_d$)** — 专业能力

由于 LLM 的 Scaling Law (Hoffmann et al., 2022) 可以评估一个 LLM 智能体的能力，我们使用它来计算 Credibility。Scaling Law 的公式为：

$$\mathcal{L}(N, M) = \frac{406.4}{N^{0.34}} + \frac{410.7}{M^{0.28}} + 1.69$$

其中 $N$、$M$ 和 $\mathcal{L}$ 分别表示模型的参数量、预训练数据的 token 数量和预训练损失。损失值越小表示模型能力越好，因此：

$$C_d = \frac{1}{\mathcal{L}(N, M)}$$

> [!warning] 易混点（非原文）
> 这里的 Scaling Law 公式来自 Chinchilla 论文 (Hoffmann et al., 2022)，是一个经验公式。它根据模型的参数量 N 和预训练数据量 M 来估计预训练损失。注意：Credibility 在辩论过程中是固定的（因为模型参数和预训练数据不变），不像其他三个因素会随辩论进行而更新。

**Reliability ($R_d$)** — 表现稳定性

代表 $A_i$ 在前 d-1 轮中对自己答案的平均置信度分数：

$$R_d = \frac{R_{d-1} \times (d-1) + H_i^{d-1}}{d}$$

**Intimacy ($I_d$)** — 观点差异程度

代表 $A_i$ 和 $A_j$ 在前 d-1 轮中观点差异的平均程度。MDM 首先使用余弦相似度计算 $O_i^{d-1}$ 和 $O_j^{d-1}$ 之间的文本相似度。然后计算平均观点相似度：

$$\overline{Sim}_d = \frac{\overline{Sim}_{d-1} \times (d-1) + cos(O_i^{d-1}, O_j^{d-1})}{d}$$

由于 $I_d$ 代表差异程度，因此：

$$I_d = 1 - \overline{Sim}_d$$

> [!tip] 理解提示（非原文）
> Intimacy 这个名字可能有些误导。在社会学原始公式中，Intimacy 指的是"亲密程度"——你和某人越亲密，越信任他。但在 MAD 适配中，论文将其重新定义为"观点差异程度"——两个智能体观点越不同，"碰撞"越多，辩论越有价值。这是 Intimacy 概念的一个巧妙转换。

**Self-Orientation ($S_d$)** — 自我导向程度

基于"较少参与群体讨论意味着更自私"的事实，MDM 使用 $A_i$ 在前 d-1 轮中与其他智能体辩论的次数 $P_d$ 来间接反映自我导向：

$$S_d = (d-1) \times (n-1) - P_d$$

其中 $(d-1) \times (n-1)$ 表示一个智能体在前 d-1 轮中最多可以与其他智能体辩论的次数。

**综合边权重计算**

遵循 McKinsey Trust Formula，边 $E_{i \to j}$ 的权重计算为：

$$W_{i \to j}^d = \frac{C_d \times R_d \times I_d}{S_d}$$

#### Step 2: 稀疏图建立

对于 $A_j$，它可以与其他 n-1 个 LLM 智能体辩论。CortexDebate 根据指向它的边的权重 $\{W_{i \to j}^d\}_{i=1, i \neq j}^n$ 来确定辩论对手集合。

首先，计算这些边的平均权重：

$$\overline{W}_j^d = \frac{1}{n-1} \sum_{i(i \neq j)} W_{i \to j}^d$$

其次，权重低于 $\overline{W}_j^d$ 的边被移除，形成稀疏辩论图：

$$W_{i \to j}^d = \begin{cases} 1, & W_{i \to j} \geq \overline{W}_j^d \\ 0, & W_{i \to j} < \overline{W}_j^d \end{cases}$$

因此，$A_j$ 的辩论对手集合为：

$$Deb_j^d = \{A_i \mid W_{i \to j}^d = 1, i \neq j\}$$

> [!note] 译者注（非原文）
> 稀疏化的策略非常简洁：对每个智能体，只保留权重高于平均值的入边。这意味着每个智能体平均只与约一半的其他智能体辩论。随着辩论进行，如果某些智能体持续表现不佳或对某个智能体没有帮助，它们之间的连接会被逐步剪除，使图越来越稀疏。

#### Step 3: 答案再生成

LLM 智能体 $A_j$ 接收 $Deb_j^d$ 中智能体在第 (d-1) 轮生成的答案，审视这些答案后生成新的答案 $O_j^d$ 和自置信度分数 $H_j^d$。输入提示可以表示为：

$$Prompt_j^d = [Ins, Q, \{O_k^{d-1}\}]$$

其中 $Ins$ 表示指示 $A_j$ 重新生成答案的指令，$\{O_k^{d-1}\}$ 表示 $A_j$ 接收到的答案集合。

#### Step 4: 辩论终止

所有 LLM 智能体生成答案后，CortexDebate 检查是否所有智能体达成共识（即所有智能体同意同一个答案）或辩论达到最大轮数。如果满足条件，辩论过程立即结束。

### 4.3 Phase 3: 最终答案生成

辩论过程结束后，CortexDebate 通过对最后一轮辩论中所有生成的答案进行多数投票来生成最终答案：

$$O_{final} = \arg\max_o \sum_i \mathbb{1}(O_i = o)$$

其中 $o$ 表示任一 LLM 智能体生成的不同答案。如果辩论后所有生成的答案都不同，我们将最终结果视为不正确。通过排除后备策略 (fallback strategies)，我们可以将观察到的性能清晰地归因于辩论机制本身。

> [!note] 译者注（非原文）
> "排除后备策略"意味着如果所有智能体的答案都不同，论文不采用额外策略（如随机选择、选择最自信的答案等），而是直接记为错误。这是一个严格但公平的评估方式，确保性能提升确实来自辩论本身。

### McKinsey Trust Formula 引入 (Section 3.2)

McKinsey Trust Formula (Lamarre et al., 2012) 在社会学中被广泛用于评估一个人在群体中的可信度水平。该公式可表示为：

$$T = \frac{C \times R \times I}{S}$$

其中 C、R、I 和 S 分别表示 Credibility（可信度）、Reliability（可靠性）、Intimacy（亲密性）和 Self-orientation（自我导向）。其中：

- **Credibility** 衡量专业能力
- **Reliability** 衡量任务表现的稳定性
- **Intimacy** 衡量与被评估者的关系
- **Self-orientation** 衡量被评估者在群体中的自我导向水平

在 MDM 模块中，我们将这四个因素适配到 MAD 场景：

| 因素 | MAD 适配含义 |
|------|-------------|
| Credibility | 评估 $A_i$ 的专业能力 |
| Reliability | $A_i$ 对自己答案的平均置信度分数，代表在当前问题上的表现可靠性 |
| Intimacy | $A_i$ 和 $A_j$ 在历史辩论中的观点差异平均程度，因为不同观点的碰撞可以增强辩论效果 (Xiong et al., 2023) |
| Self-orientation | $A_i$ 在辩论中的参与水平（参与水平越低，自我导向越高） |

## 关键要点回顾

- 辩论图是有向的，边权重反映"一个智能体对另一个智能体的预期帮助程度"
- 置信度重校准策略防止极端高/低置信度
- MDM 模块通过 McKinsey Trust Formula 的四个因素综合评估边权重
- 稀疏化策略简洁有效：移除权重低于平均值的边
- 辩论终止条件：共识达成或达到最大轮数

## 下一步学习

# CortexDebate — McKinsey Trust Formula 与 MDM 模块详解

> 本篇深入分析 McKinsey Trust Formula 在 CortexDebate 中的具体适配，以及消融实验中各组件的贡献分析。

## 正文翻译

### McKinsey Trust Formula 的原始含义与 MAD 适配

McKinsey Trust Formula (Lamarre et al., 2012) 原始用于社会学，衡量一个人在群体中的可信度。公式形式为：

$$T = \frac{C \times R \times I}{S}$$

这个公式的直觉含义：

- **分子越大越可信**：专业能力强 (C)、表现稳定可靠 (R)、与你关系亲密 (I)
- **分母越小越可信**：自我导向程度低 (S)，即这个人更多地为团队考虑而非只顾自己

### MDM 模块中四个因素的深度解读

#### 1. Credibility — 基于 Scaling Law 的能力评估

MDM 使用 Chinchilla Scaling Law (Hoffmann et al., 2022) 来计算 Credibility：

$$\mathcal{L}(N, M) = \frac{406.4}{N^{0.34}} + \frac{410.7}{M^{0.28}} + 1.69$$

$$C_d = \frac{1}{\mathcal{L}(N, M)}$$

这个公式的含义是：给定一个模型的参数量 N 和预训练数据量 M，可以估算其预训练损失 $\mathcal{L}$。预训练损失越小（模型越强），$C_d$ 越大。

> [!tip] 理解提示（非原文）
> 具体例子：假设模型 A 有 7B 参数、预训练 1T tokens，模型 B 有 9B 参数、预训练 2T tokens。通过 Scaling Law 公式可以计算出各自的预训练损失，进而得到 Credibility 分数。这个分数在辩论过程中是固定的，因为模型参数和预训练数据不变。

#### 2. Reliability — 历史置信度的递推平均

$$R_d = \frac{R_{d-1} \times (d-1) + H_i^{d-1}}{d}$$

这是一个递推平均值——每轮辩论后，将新获得的置信度分数纳入历史平均。它反映的是 $A_i$ 在当前问题上对自己答案的"一贯自信程度"。

一个总是给出高置信度的智能体会有高 Reliability，但如果它的答案经常改变（置信度波动大），Reliability 也会受到影响。

#### 3. Intimacy — 观点碰撞的价值

$$\overline{Sim}_d = \frac{\overline{Sim}_{d-1} \times (d-1) + cos(O_i^{d-1}, O_j^{d-1})}{d}$$

$$I_d = 1 - \overline{Sim}_d$$

> [!warning] 易混点（非原文）
> Intimacy 在社会学的原始含义是"亲密程度"——你越信任亲密的人。但论文将这个概念做了反向适配：**Intimacy 越高 = 观点差异越大 = 辩论越有价值**。
>
> 这是因为在辩论场景中，如果两个智能体总是给出相同的答案，辩论就没有意义；只有在观点不同时，"碰撞"才能带来信息增益。论文引用 Xiong et al. (2023) 的工作支持这一观点。

Intimacy 的计算使用余弦相似度：首先将两个智能体的输出用嵌入模型（论文使用 OpenAI 的 text-embedding-3-large）编码为向量，然后计算余弦相似度。$I_d = 1 - \overline{Sim}_d$ 确保观点越不同，Intimacy 越高。

#### 4. Self-Orientation — 参与度作为自私的代理指标

$$S_d = (d-1) \times (n-1) - P_d$$

其中 $(d-1) \times (n-1)$ 是最大可能的辩论次数（前 d-1 轮，每轮最多与 n-1 个其他智能体辩论），$P_d$ 是实际辩论次数。

> [!note] 译者注（非原文）
> Self-Orientation 作为分母，意味着参与辩论越多的智能体 S 越小，边权重越大。这是合理的：积极参与群体讨论的智能体更"无私"，其观点更值得参考。
>
> 注意 $P_d$ 是由稀疏图的剪枝结果决定的——被剪枝越多的智能体，辩论次数越少，S 越大，在后续轮次中更可能被继续剪枝。这形成了一个正反馈：积极参与 → 被保留 → 继续参与。

### 消融实验：每个组件的贡献

论文通过详细的消融实验验证每个组件的不可或缺性（八数据集平均分数）：

| 方法配置 | 平均分数 (%) | 分析 |
|----------|-------------|------|
| 全连接图 | 60.49 | 基础 MAD 框架 |
| 全连接图 + MDM | 63.76 | MDM 有帮助，但上下文仍然过长 |
| 稀疏图 | 62.72 | 稀疏图本身有帮助（减少信息过载） |
| 稀疏图 + 自评估 (RECONCILE) | 62.13 | 仅靠自评估会加剧过度自信 |
| 稀疏图 + 同行评估 (PRD) | 66.71 | 同行评估缓解过度自信 |
| 稀疏图 + MDM (无 I 和 S) | 66.69 | 仅考虑个人能力因素 |
| **稀疏图 + MDM (完整)** | **69.41** | 同时考虑个人能力和协作因素 |

> [!tip] 理解提示（非原文）
> 消融实验揭示了几个关键洞察：
> 1. 稀疏图 vs 全连接图：从 63.76 到 69.41，说明减少上下文长度本身就能显著提升性能
> 2. 自评估 vs 同行评估：62.13 vs 66.71，说明仅靠自身置信度来决定影响力是有害的
> 3. MDM (无 I/S) vs MDM (完整)：66.69 vs 69.41，说明考虑智能体间的交互关系（观点差异和参与度）是关键的提升来源

### Intimacy 和 Self-Orientation 的量化效果

论文在 MATH 数据集上对比了有无 I 和 S 因子的交互质量：

| 配置 | DVC（不同观点碰撞次数） | CVR（正确观点修正次数） | CVR/DVC (%) |
|------|----------------------|----------------------|-------------|
| 无 I 和 S 因子 | 3.71 | 1.26 | 33.96 |
| 有 I 和 S 因子 | 8.44 | 4.83 | **64.92** |

- **DVC (Different Viewpoint Collisions)**：每题平均不同观点碰撞次数
- **CVR (Correct Viewpoint Revisions)**：每题平均正确观点修正次数
- **CVR/DVC**：碰撞转化为正确修正的比率

> [!note] 译者注（非原文）
> 这组数据非常有说服力：加入 I 和 S 因子后，不仅观点碰撞的频率增加（3.71 → 8.44），更重要的是碰撞的"质量"大幅提升（转化率从 33.96% 翻倍到 64.92%）。这说明 MDM 不仅鼓励更多辩论，还确保辩论更有效——智能体更有可能在碰撞中修正自己的错误观点。

### Edge Pruning 策略对比（附录 D）

论文在 MATH 数据集上比较了不同的边剪枝策略：

| 策略 | RA (%) | 描述 |
|------|--------|------|
| Top-3 | 54.00 | 只与权重最高的 3 个智能体辩论 |
| Bot-3 | 56.00 | 排除权重最低的 3 个智能体 |
| AMT | 52.00 | 中位数阈值（高于中位数的保留） |
| **AAT (Ours)** | **57.00** | **平均阈值（高于平均值的保留）** |

平均阈值 (AAT) 策略表现最好，因为它可以根据每轮的实际权重分布自适应地决定保留多少边。

### 置信度重校准的鲁棒性（附录 D.3）

论文测试了不同阈值配置对性能的影响：

| 阈值配置 | 平均分数 (%) |
|----------|-------------|
| 无重校准 | 68.62 |
| [0.9, 0.7, 0.2] | 68.96 |
| [0.9, 0.6, 0.3] | 69.03 |
| [0.8, 0.6, 0.2] | 69.15 |
| [0.8, 0.5, 0.1] | 68.84 |
| [0.7, 0.5, 0.2] | 68.72 |
| **[0.8, 0.6, 0.3] (Ours)** | **69.41** |

论文指出，该方法对不同阈值配置总体上不太敏感（范围在 68.62 到 69.41 之间），说明重校准策略具有鲁棒性。

### 文本相似度计算方法对比（附录 D.2）

在 LongBench 数据集上比较了不同的文本相似度计算方法：

| 方法 | M-Avg (%) |
|------|-----------|
| DeTS (NLI 训练模型) | 57.57 ± 0.69 |
| DeepSeek R1 + prompt | 56.91 ± 3.10 |
| **余弦相似度 (Ours)** | **60.31 ± 0.32** |

论文指出，使用大语言模型进行文本相似度估计往往不稳定（表现为更高的方差），而简单的余弦相似度方法反而更高效、更稳定。

## 关键要点回顾

- McKinsey Trust Formula 的四个因素在 MAD 场景中被精心适配
- Credibility 固定不变（基于模型参数），其他三个因素动态更新
- 消融实验证明：稀疏图和 MDM 模块缺一不可，Intimacy 和 Self-Orientation 因子带来约 2.7% 的额外提升
- I 和 S 因子将观点碰撞的修正率从 33.96% 提升到 64.92%
- 平均阈值 (AAT) 剪枝策略优于固定数量和中位数策略
- 余弦相似度比 LLM-based 相似度估计更高效稳定

## 下一步学习

# CortexDebate — 实验 (Section 5: Experiments)

> 本篇翻译论文第 5 节 Experiments 的内容，包括实验设置、主要结果和深入分析。

## 正文翻译

### 5.1 实验设置

#### 任务与数据集

实验考虑了四类典型任务：

| 任务类型 | 数据集 | 描述 |
|----------|--------|------|
| **数学** | GSM-IC | 基于 GSM8K 的中小学数学题，每题添加一句无关干扰信息 |
| **数学** | MATH | 包含挑战性竞赛数学问题的数据集，每题有完整步骤解答 |
| **世界知识 QA** | MMLU | 57 类多项选择题（初等数学、美国历史、计算机科学等） |
| **世界知识 QA** | MMLU-pro | 题源更多元（MMLU、TheoremQA、SciBench），选项从 4 个扩展到 10 个 |
| **推理** | GPQA | 448 道研究生级别问答题，涵盖生物、物理、化学 |
| **推理** | ARC-C | 自然科学领域的复杂选择题 |
| **长上下文理解** | LongBench | 评估长上下文理解能力，涵盖 6 大类任务 |
| **长上下文理解** | SQuAD | 评估阅读理解能力，需从给定长文本中回答不同问题 |

#### 评估指标

| 数据集 | 指标 | 说明 |
|--------|------|------|
| LongBench | M-Avg (Macro-Average) | 各主要子任务类别的平均分 |
| SQuAD | EM (Exact Match) | 输出包含正确答案的百分比 |
| 其余 6 个数据集 | RA (Result Accuracy) | 正确结果的百分比 |

#### 基线方法

三类基线方法：

1. **无辩论 (No Debate)**：
   - **Multi-agent Voting (MaV)** (Wang et al., 2022)：多智能体多数投票

2. **全辩论 (Full Debate)**：
   - **Multi-LLM Debate (MLD)** (Du et al., 2023)：基本的多 LLM 辩论
   - **RECONCILE** (Chen et al., 2023)：添加置信度分数的辩论
   - **ChatEval** (Chan et al., 2023)：使用额外 LLM 总结辩论结果
   - **Peer Review Debate (PRD)** (Xu et al., 2023b)：同行评审策略辩论

3. **部分辩论 (Part Debate)**：
   - **GroupDebate (GD)** (Liu et al., 2024b)：分组辩论
   - **Neighbor Debate (ND)** (Li et al., 2024b)：仅与邻居辩论

> [!note] 译者注（非原文）
> 这三分类法很清晰：无辩论是"各自为政，投票汇总"；全辩论是"所有人与所有人辩论"（全连接图）；部分辩论是"只与部分人辩论"（稀疏但固定拓扑）。CortexDebate 属于第三类但拓扑是动态优化的。

#### 骨干模型

| 简称 | 模型 | 参数量 |
|------|------|--------|
| Qwen | Qwen-2.5-7B-Instruct-Turbo | 7B |
| Mistral | Mistral-7B-Instruct | 7B |
| Typhoon | Typhoon-1.5-8B-Instruct | 8B |
| Llama | Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct-Turbo | 8B |
| Gemma | Gemma-2-9B-Instruct | 9B |

#### 实现细节

- 遵循先前工作 (Du et al., 2023; Chen et al., 2023; Besta et al., 2024)，在每个数据集的 100 个样本子集上实验
- 每个实验在相同样本上运行 3 次，报告平均结果和方差
- 所有辩论方法的最大辩论轮数设为 5
- 在更具挑战性的数据集（MATH、MMLU-pro、GPQA、LongBench）上进行了 1000 样本的大规模实验

### 5.2 主要结果

#### CortexDebate 优于基线方法

Table 1 报告了 CortexDebate 和基线方法在八个数据集上的准确率。与基线方法相比，CortexDebate 在所有采用的数据集上实现了最高准确率，且表现稳定。

此外，我们可以发现全辩论方法（即 MLD、RECONCILE、ChatEval 和 PRD）在复杂推理和长上下文任务（即 GPQA、LongBench 和 SQuAD）上的有效性和稳定性下降。这是因为推理过程随任务复杂度增加而变长，导致第 1 节提到的冗长上下文问题。然而，CortexDebate 仍然表现良好且稳定，这归功于其稀疏辩论图减少了输入上下文长度，以及 MDM 模块使每个 LLM 智能体与那些对自己有帮助的智能体辩论。

**主实验结果（100 样本）：**

| 类型 | 方法 | GSM-IC | MATH | MMLU | MMLU-pro | GPQA | ARC-C | LongBench | SQuAD |
|------|------|--------|------|------|----------|------|-------|-----------|-------|
| 无辩论 | MaV | 70.33 | 46.00 | 69.33 | 46.00 | 27.33 | 76.00 | 45.11 | 85.33 |
| 全辩论 | MLD | 72.67 | 47.33 | 71.33 | 47.33 | 28.33 | 79.33 | 48.87 | 86.33 |
| 全辩论 | RECONCILE | 75.67 | 50.33 | 75.00 | 53.67 | 31.00 | 83.67 | 52.55 | 88.33 |
| 全辩论 | ChatEval | 74.33 | 49.00 | 73.00 | 49.33 | 31.33 | 82.67 | 53.56 | 87.33 |
| 全辩论 | PRD | 77.00 | 51.33 | 77.33 | 54.00 | 32.00 | 84.33 | 50.21 | 87.67 |
| 部分辩论 | GD | 76.00 | 49.67 | 74.00 | 51.67 | 32.67 | 82.00 | 55.97 | 90.33 |
| 部分辩论 | ND | 73.67 | 49.00 | 71.67 | 48.67 | 32.33 | 81.33 | 54.55 | 88.33 |
| **本文** | **CortexDebate** | **79.33** | **56.00** | **82.33** | **59.33** | **36.33** | **88.33** | **60.31** | **93.33** |

> [!tip] 理解提示（非原文）
> 注意 CortexDebate 在 ARC-C 上达到 88.33%（比 MaV 的 76.00% 提升了 12.33%），在 SQuAD 上达到 93.33%（比 MaV 的 85.33% 提升了 8.00%）。在 MMLU-pro 上更是达到 59.33%，远超次优的 PRD（54.00%），提升了 5.33%。

#### CortexDebate 显著减少输入上下文长度

对于每个采用的数据集，我们计算了每种方法中单个 LLM 智能体的平均输入 token 数。

**关键数据：输入上下文长度缩减**

- 相比全辩论方法，CortexDebate 最大缩减 **70.79%** 的输入上下文长度
- 相比部分辩论方法（GD、ND），CortexDebate 至少减少 **17.62%** 的输入上下文长度

> [!note] 译者注（非原文）
> 这是 CortexDebate 最重要的附加价值之一：不仅在准确率上领先，还在效率上大幅提升。这意味着 CortexDebate 可以在更低的 API 成本下运行（输入 token 数减少），同时获得更好的结果——真正实现了"少即是多"。

#### CortexDebate 辩论有效且平等

**辩论效果随轮次提升（Figure 4a）：**

- 随着辩论进行，CortexDebate 的性能持续提升
- CortexDebate 保持优越性能，最终达到最高分 69.41%

**共识比例变化（Figure 4b）：**

- 在初始轮次中，由于 CortexDebate 鼓励不同观点的平等碰撞，其共识比例相对较低
- 随着辩论进行，共识比例逐步提升至高水平
- 相比之下，RECONCILE 保持最高的共识比例但分数波动——这是由于过度自信导致的不平等辩论，辩论被少数 LLM 智能体主导，其他智能体倾向于"投降"
- CortexDebate 缓解了这个问题，在分数和共识比例上都实现了持续增长

**各轮次平均分数与共识比例（Table 8 部分数据）：**

| 方法 | Round 1 | Round 2 | Round 3 | Round 4 | Round 5 |
|------|---------|---------|---------|---------|---------|
| **CortexDebate 分数** | **60.31** | **65.65** | **67.63** | **69.10** | **69.41** |
| RECONCILE 分数 | 58.95 | 60.71 | 67.30 | 65.64 | 63.65 |
| **CortexDebate 共识** | 51.75 | 62.13 | 76.75 | 89.63 | 95.50 |
| RECONCILE 共识 | 73.63 | 79.25 | 94.00 | 95.75 | 98.63 |

> [!warning] 易混点（非原文）
> RECONCILE 的共识比例很高（98.63%），但最终分数反而下降（从 67.30% 降到 63.65%）。这说明高共识不等于高质量——如果辩论被过度自信的智能体主导，"共识"可能是错的共识。CortexDebate 的共识比例逐步提升到 95.50% 的同时，分数也持续提升到 69.41%，说明它的共识是"健康的"——通过充分的观点碰撞逐步达成正确共识。

### 5.3 性能深入分析

#### CortexDebate 保留有益辩论

在 MATH 数据集上，论文计算了每个参与智能体在全连接图 (FcG) 和 CortexDebate 下的平均辩论次数 (Avg-DN) 和 RA：

| 智能体 | FcG RA (%) | FcG Avg-DN | CortexDebate RA (%) | CortexDebate Avg-DN |
|--------|-----------|-----------|--------------------|--------------------|
| Qwen | 54.00 | 13.58 | **58.00** | 11.53 |
| Mistral | 49.00 | 13.58 | **56.00** | 6.24 |
| Typhoon | 47.00 | 13.58 | **53.00** | 8.83 |
| Llama | 51.00 | 13.58 | **56.00** | 11.37 |
| Gemma | 45.00 | 13.58 | **51.00** | 5.46 |

> [!tip] 理解提示（非原文）
> 这组数据非常直观地展示了 CortexDebate 的效果：每个智能体的辩论次数都减少了（从固定的 13.58 降到 5.46-11.53 不等），但每个智能体的准确率反而都提升了。这说明 CortexDebate 确实在"剪除有害辩论，保留有益辩论"。
>
> 特别值得注意的是 Mistral 和 Gemma 的辩论次数大幅减少（从 13.58 降到 6.24 和 5.46），说明这两个模型在稀疏图中被认为是"对其他人帮助有限"的辩论对手，但它们自身的准确率反而提升了——因为它们接收到的是更精炼、更有帮助的辩论信息。

#### CortexDebate 在大规模辩论中表现优异

论文探索了 LLM 智能体数量和辩论轮数对 CortexDebate 的影响：

- 随着智能体数量和辩论轮数的增加，CortexDebate 的任务性能持续提升
- 与辩论轮数相比，LLM 智能体数量的增加对 CortexDebate 的性能提升贡献更大
- 这证明了 CortexDebate 在大规模辩论中的应用潜力

#### 大规模实验结果（1000 样本）

| 方法 | MATH RA (%) | MMLU-pro M-Avg (%) | GPQA RA (%) | LongBench M-Avg (%) |
|------|-----------|-------------------|-----------|-------------------|
| MaV | 47.40 | 46.30 | 29.10 | 43.35 |
| MLD | 49.20 | 48.40 | 30.60 | 46.26 |
| RECONCILE | 50.70 | 53.10 | 30.80 | 48.33 |
| ChatEval | 49.90 | 49.30 | 31.10 | 51.23 |
| PRD | 51.20 | 54.20 | 32.40 | 47.67 |
| GD | 50.30 | 51.30 | 34.20 | 54.58 |
| ND | 49.50 | 49.10 | 32.80 | 54.14 |
| **CortexDebate** | **56.30** | **58.90** | **36.60** | **59.63** |

大规模实验结果与 100 样本实验一致，CortexDebate 在所有数据集上都取得了最佳性能。

**大规模实验的输入上下文长度缩减：**

- 相比全辩论方法，最大缩减 **65.50%**
- 相比部分辩论方法，至少缩减 **17.40%**

## 关键要点回顾

- CortexDebate 在 8 个数据集上全面领先，平均分 69.41%（次优 66.71%）
- 输入上下文长度最大缩减 70.79%，同时保持更高准确率
- RECONCILE 的高共识比例伴随分数下降，说明"高共识不等于高质量"
- 每个智能体在辩论次数减少的同时准确率提升——证明稀疏图保留了有益辩论
- 智能体数量增加比辩论轮数增加对性能提升贡献更大
- 大规模实验（1000 样本）结果与 100 样本实验一致

## 下一步学习

# CortexDebate — 结论与局限性 (Section 6: Conclusion & Limitations)

> 本篇翻译论文第 6 节 Conclusion 和 Limitations 的内容。

## 正文翻译

### 6 结论

在本文中，我们提出了一种新的 MAD 方法——"CortexDebate"，以提升多智能体交互系统的推理能力。具体来说，我们的 CortexDebate 在参与的 LLM 智能体之间建立了稀疏辩论图，减少了 LLM 智能体的输入信息负担。此外，通过整合 McKinsey Trust Formula，我们提出的 MDM 模块进行可信评估，逐步优化辩论图，使辩论过程更加平等、深入和有效。

由于上述设计，我们的方法缓解了现有 MAD 系统面临的两大主要问题（即过长的输入上下文和过度自信导致的不平等辩论），并在各种典型任务上展现出优于各种最先进 MAD 方法的性能。

在未来，我们计划继续探索 CortexDebate 在大规模辩论和复杂任务（即领域专家系统）中的潜力。

### 局限性

尽管 CortexDebate 展现了令人印象深刻的性能，我们承认它存在两个主要局限：

**局限 1：效率与成本。** 作为一种多智能体辩论方法，与单智能体方法相比，在解决任务时不可避免地会出现效率下降和成本增加。

**局限 2：受限于底层 LLM 的推理能力。** 尽管取得了成功，LLM 智能体的推理能力仍然是限制 CortexDebate 性能的重要因素。虽然 CortexDebate 改进了 LLM 智能体之间的辩论策略，但由于 LLM 智能体推理能力不足，错误仍可能发生。

> [!note] 译者注（非原文）
> 这两个局限性都是 MAD 方法固有的通病：
> 1. 多智能体必然需要多次推理调用，成本是单智能体的 n 倍。不过 CortexDebate 的稀疏图在一定程度上缓解了这个问题——不是每次都与所有人辩论，减少了输入 token 数。
> 2. "垃圾进，垃圾出"——如果所有底层 LLM 都缺乏某个领域的推理能力，再好的辩论策略也无法凭空产生正确答案。辩论的优势在于"集合智慧"，但当所有智能体都不擅长某个问题时，集合智慧也无济于事。

### 论文核心 Prompt 设计（附录 F）

论文提供了 CortexDebate 使用的两种 Prompt：

**初始答案生成 Prompt：**

```
Question: {问题描述}
Please think it step by step and generate an answer and an explanation for your answer.
Also, evaluate how confident you are that your answer is correct.
Your confidence score should between 0 and 1.
The format of your answer must be:
Answer: (...)
Explanation: (...)
Confidence Score: (...)
```

**答案再生成 Prompt：**

```
Question: {问题描述}
There are some answers generated by other LLM agents:
One LLM agent answer: {答案}
One LLM agent answer: {答案}
... ...
Using these answers as additional information, please generate a new answer
and an explanation for your answer.
Also, evaluate how confident you are that your answer is correct.
Your confidence score should between 0 and 1.
The format of your answer must be:
Answer: (...)
Explanation: (...)
Confidence Score: (...)
```

> [!tip] 理解提示（非原文）
> Prompt 设计相对简洁。关键点在于：答案再生成时，智能体只接收稀疏图中相连的对手的答案（不是所有人的答案）。这减少了输入信息量，同时也更聚焦——只看"对自己有帮助的"对手的观点。

### 基线方法详细介绍（附录 G）

**Multi-agent Voting (MaV)**：每个智能体独立生成答案，通过多数投票决定最终预测。

**ChatEval**：使用额外的 LLM 智能体在每轮辩论中总结辩论结果。总结文本作为下一轮辩论的补充信息输入给每个智能体。

**Multi-LLM Debate (MLD)**：首先每个 LLM 生成答案。然后每个 LLM 阅读并批评其他智能体的答案，生成新答案。此步骤重复多次。最终通过多数投票获得答案。

**RECONCILE**：给定问题，每个 LLM 首先生成答案和不确定性。然后所有智能体进入多轮辩论，每轮基于上一轮所有其他智能体的答案生成修正答案和新的不确定性。最终通过多数投票获得答案。

**Peer Review Debate (PRD)**：类似 RECONCILE，但采用同行评审策略——每个智能体的答案由其他智能体评分。

**GroupDebate (GD)**：将智能体分为若干辩论组，每组进行内部辩论。内部辩论后，每组的结果被汇总放入共享池。每组从池中检索所有组的辩论总结，作为下一轮所有智能体的输入。

**Neighbor Debate (ND)**：LLM 仅与其邻居辩论（拓扑固定）。

### 致谢

本研究得到了中国国家自然科学基金（Nos: 62336003, 12371510）和江苏省自然科学基金（No: BK20241469）的支持。

## 整体评价与思考

### 论文的亮点

1. **生物学启发的类比非常有说服力**：将 LLM 智能体对应为皮层区域，MDM 对应为白质，稀疏辩论图对应为皮层间稀疏网络，这个类比既直观又有理论支撑。

2. **McKinsey Trust Formula 的引入是关键创新**：将社会学中的信任评估公式适配到 MAD 场景，同时考虑个体能力和协作效果，有效解决了过度自信问题。

3. **实验非常全面**：4 类任务、8 个数据集、多种基线方法、100 样本和 1000 样本两个规模、详细的消融实验和深入分析。

4. **"少即是多"的验证**：减少辩论对手反而提升性能，这一反直觉的发现很有价值。

### 论文的不足

1. **未在闭源大模型上实验**：仅使用 7B-9B 开源模型，未验证在 GPT-4、Claude 等更大模型上是否仍有显著提升。

2. **缺乏效率与成本的量化分析**：虽然展示了输入 token 数减少，但未提供总 API 调用成本、端到端运行时间等实用性指标。

3. **稀疏图动态性的分析不够深入**：论文展示了动态优化稀疏图的效果，但未分析辩论图在多轮辩论中的演化过程——哪些连接被保留？哪些被移除？为什么？

4. **对"平等辩论"的衡量标准有限**：论文主要通过共识比例和分数变化来论证辩论的"平等性"，但缺乏对每个智能体话语权变化的具体分析。

## 关键要点回顾

- CortexDebate 通过稀疏辩论图和 MDM 模块有效解决了 MAD 的两大核心问题
- 两个主要局限：效率/成本和受限于底层 LLM 能力
- 未来方向：大规模辩论和领域专家系统
- Prompt 设计简洁，易于复现

160	- [[raw/lessons/LangGraph/07-streaming|第 7 章：流式执行——实时看见研究助手正在如何思考与推进]] — `stream()`、`stream_mode`、`version="v2"`、执行过程可观测性
161	- [[raw/lessons/LangGraph/08-send-and-parallel|第 8 章：并行与分发——让研究助手同时处理多个检索任务]] — `Send`、动态扇出、聚合规则、最小 map-reduce 风格
162	- [[raw/lessons/LangGraph/09-subgraphs-and-composition|第 9 章：子图与组合——把研究助手拆成可复用模块]] — 子图接入父图、共享状态、私有状态、模块化 Research Copilot
163	- [[raw/lessons/LangGraph/10-deployment-and-beyond|第 10 章：部署与扩展——把研究助手整理成本地可运行应用]] — `langgraph dev`、`langgraph.json`、应用结构整理、Functional API 视角
164	
165	### LangGraph 到 Harness 桥接系列（2 篇）
166	
167	- [[raw/lessons/LangGraph-to-Harness/01-from-graph-to-runtime|第 1 章：从 Graph 到 Runtime——为什么下一步是 LangChain 1.x 与 Harness]] — 从 LangGraph 课程过渡到 LangChain 1.x 与 Claude Code-like harness 的系统桥接，总结你已掌握的 runtime 骨架，并明确后续双轨路线
168	- [[raw/lessons/LangGraph-to-Harness/02-from-primitives-to-system-modules|第 2 章：从原语到系统模块——LangGraph 能力如何落到真实 Agent 架构]] — 把 state、node、edge、checkpoint、interrupt、subgraph 等 LangGraph 原语稳定映射到 session、planner、executor、permission gate、review 等真实系统模块
169	
170	### LangChain 1.x 系统学习（6 篇）
171	
172	- [[raw/lessons/LangChain/01-why-langchain-1-x|第 1 章：为什么 LangChain 1.x 是下一步]] — 明确后续只以 LangChain 1.x 为主线，理解它在模型、消息、工具、结构化输出与 agent abstraction 上的标准组件层价值
173	- [[raw/lessons/LangChain/02-langchain-1-x-core-abstractions|第 2 章：LangChain 1.x 的分层结构与核心抽象]] — 建立 model、messages、tools、structured output、agent abstraction 的统一组件地图，并明确它们在 Harness 中的系统落点
174	- [[raw/lessons/LangChain/03-models-messages-and-provider-boundaries|第 3 章：Models、Messages 与 Provider Boundaries]] — 讲清模型接口、消息对象和 provider 差异边界，帮助后续做干净的 provider adapter 与 conversation state
175	- [[raw/lessons/LangChain/04-tools-and-structured-output|第 4 章：Tools 与 Structured Output——让结果可调用、可验证、可组合]] — 讲清工具调用协议、ToolMessage 回写、schema 思维、provider-native structured output 与 tool strategy 的系统意义
176	- [[raw/lessons/LangChain/05-prompt-and-context-organization|第 5 章：Prompt 与上下文组织——什么应放在哪一层]] — 讲清模型真正看见的是哪些消息、runtime context 为什么默认不可见，以及 system / task / history / tool results 该如何分层组织，才能稳定支撑 tool calling 与 structured output
177	- [[raw/lessons/LangChain/06-memory-state-history-boundaries|第 6 章：Memory / State / History——哪些属于组件层，哪些属于系统层]] — 讲清 messages history、conversation state 与 cross-conversation store 的边界，明确 short-term memory 更接近 state，long-term memory 更接近 store，以及哪些职责属于 LangChain 组件层、哪些仍要交给 harness 决策
178	
179	### Agent Engineering 与 Claude Code-like Harness（8 篇）
180	
181	- [[raw/lessons/Agent-Engineering/01-claude-code-like-systems-overview|第 1 章：Claude Code-like 系统总览]] — 从公开能力面拆解 coding agent 的最小模块，明确 MVP / intermediate / advanced 三层工程路线
182	- [[raw/lessons/Agent-Engineering/02-harness-mvp-architecture|第 2 章：Harness MVP 架构——一个最小 coding agent 系统怎么拆]] — 讲清最小 coding agent 的骨架：交互、session、planner、executor、tools、permission、review、logging、config 为什么缺一不可
183	- [[raw/lessons/Agent-Engineering/03-planner-executor-reviewer-loop|第 3 章：Planner / Executor / Reviewer——受控执行回路是怎样形成的]] — 讲清任务如何在规划、执行、复查之间形成可纠偏的主回路，并说明 permission gate 在其中的闸门位置
184	- [[raw/lessons/Agent-Engineering/04-tool-layer-read-search-edit-run|第 4 章：工具层——读文件、搜代码、编辑文件、运行命令为什么构成最小 action surface]] — 把 `read / search / edit / run` 解释为 coding agent 的最小动作面，并说明为什么工具层是系统对外动作的法定出口
185	- [[raw/lessons/Agent-Engineering/05-permissions-and-confirmation-boundaries|第 5 章：权限与确认边界——为什么高风险动作不能直接放行]] — 讲清 allow / ask / deny 三类决策、确认机制的中断语义，以及权限边界如何决定 agent 的行动权
186	- [[raw/lessons/Agent-Engineering/06-session-memory-checkpoint-resume|第 6 章：Session、Memory、Checkpoint、Resume——为什么不能把状态全塞进一个记忆盒子]] — 区分会话状态、长期记忆、可恢复快照与恢复流程，避免把所有状态混成一个大容器
187	- [[raw/lessons/Agent-Engineering/07-task-decomposition-and-multi-step-strategy|第 7 章：任务分解与多步执行策略——复杂任务为什么不能只靠临场乱试]] — 讲清任务形状判断、顺序 / 分层 / 条件分支拆解、局部上下文打包，以及 retry / 局部补救 / replan 怎样在真实 harness 中协同发生
188	- [[raw/lessons/Agent-Engineering/08-project-understanding-and-repo-exploration|第 8 章：项目理解与仓库探索——为什么 agent 不能一上来就盲改代码]] — 讲清目录结构扫描、模式发现、局部精读三层仓库探索策略，以及探索结果如何被压缩成后续步骤可复用的项目上下文对象
189

684	## [2026-04-15] research | Agent 蜂群动态协作拓扑——调研与创新架构设计
685	
686	用户提出"做一个 Agent 蜂群项目能否发论文"的想法。经过全面调研和严格审查，完成了创新架构设计。
687	
688	**调研范围**（10+ 论文/框架）：
689	- 经典框架：MetaGPT (ICLR 2024), AutoGen (Microsoft), ChatDev
690	- 协作机制：MachineSoM (社会心理学视角), Multi-Agent Collaboration Survey (arXiv:2501.06322)
691	- 失败分析：MAST (NeurIPS 2025, 14种失败模式)
692	- 群体智能+LLM：Model Swarms (Google, 权重层), MASOIE (算法层)
693	- 安全与拓扑：NetSafe (拓扑安全性), AgentCoord (可视化协作)
694	- 自组织：SoA (自组织Agent), MDTeamGPT (自进化医疗框架)
695	
696	**识别的研究空白**：
697	1. **拓扑是静态的**（核心Gap）— 所有框架使用预定义固定拓扑，无人研究任务驱动的动态适配
698	2. **群体智能"行为层"空白** — 权重层和算法层已探索，行为层完全空白
699	3. **失败诊断了但没治疗** — MAST 停留在诊断层面
700	
701	**提出架构：SwarmTopo v2**（已通过 architect 严格审查）：
702	- 环境信号层（解决LLM自评校准性问题）
703	- 软拓扑适配（连续参数空间，解决离散跳跃问题）
704	- 改进版状态机（补充缺失路径）
705	- Coordinator轮值制（解决冷启动和单点故障）
706	
707	**审查反馈整合**：
708	- ⚠️ 致命风险：创新性可能被占先（需第1-2周严格文献搜索确认）
709	- ⚠️ 关键改进：新增消融A4（vs简单规则），叙事重心从"群体智能"转向"动态拓扑适配"
710	- ✅ 可行性：API成本$30-50可控，3-4个月时间可行
711

1	# Wiki 索引
2	
3	> 本文件是 Wiki 的内容目录，LLM 每次摄入时更新。
4	
5	## 统计
6	
7	| 类型 | 数量 |
8	|------|------|
9	| 概念页 | 19 |
10	| 实体页 | 9 |
11	| 摘要页 | 0 |
12	| 对比页 | 5 |
13	| 概述页 | 3 |
14	| 学习笔记 | 151 |
15	
16	---
17	
18	## 概念（concepts/）
19	
20	### Transformer 核心组件
21	- [[wiki/concepts/self-attention]] — 序列内部各位置互相关注的基础注意力机制
22	- [[wiki/concepts/multi-head-attention]] — 多视角并行关注，拆分为多个低维子空间捕捉不同依赖关系
23	- [[wiki/concepts/positional-encoding]] — 通过数学函数向词嵌入注入位置信号，解决 Self-Attention 置换等变性问题
24	- [[wiki/concepts/feed-forward-network]] — Position-wise 前馈网络，对每个 token 独立做非线性特征提取，占 Transformer 约 2/3 参数
25	- [[wiki/concepts/residual-connection]] — 绕过非线性变换的梯度高速公路，让深层网络可训练
26	- [[wiki/concepts/layer-normalization]] — 逐样本归一化，稳定深层网络的数值分布
27	
28	### 编码器-解码器架构
29	- [[wiki/concepts/encoder-decoder]] — Transformer 核心架构范式：编码器理解输入，解码器生成输出
30	- [[wiki/concepts/cross-attention]] — 连接编码器与解码器的桥梁，Q 来自解码器、K/V 来自编码器的跨序列注意力
31	- [[wiki/concepts/masked-attention]] — 因果遮罩机制，屏蔽未来位置确保自回归生成的因果性
32	
33	### 训练与推理
34	- [[wiki/concepts/autoregressive-generation]] — 语言模型逐 token 生成的核心范式：每步输出成为下步输入，直到生成结束符
35	- [[wiki/concepts/beam-search]] — 自回归生成中选择下一个 token 的解码策略对比：贪心、Beam Search、Top-k、Top-p、Temperature
36	- [[wiki/concepts/kv-cache]] — 缓存 Key-Value 向量避免重复计算，将推理复杂度从 O(T²) 降至 O(T)
37	
38	### 现代扩展
39	- [[wiki/concepts/scaling-law]] — 模型性能随参数规模、数据量和计算量的幂律关系增长，大模型涌现全新能力
40	- [[wiki/concepts/mixture-of-experts]] — 通过稀疏激活机制，用少量计算量驱动庞大参数空间的 MoE 架构
41	
42	### LangGraph / Agent 工作流
43	- [[wiki/concepts/state-graph]] — LangGraph 最核心的图构建原语：围绕共享状态组织节点、边与执行流程
44	- [[wiki/concepts/langgraph-api-map]] — 面向系统学习的 LangGraph API 路线图：从 `StateGraph` 到 `langgraph dev` 的能力地图
45	
46	### 知识库维护
47	- [[wiki/concepts/series-navigation-consistency]] — 系列课程的章节顺序、导航链接、路线图一致性检查清单与错误预防
48	
49	### Wiki 方法论
50	- [[wiki/concepts/knowledge-compilation]] — 让 LLM 一次性编译原始资料为结构化知识，而非每次重新检索
51	- [[wiki/concepts/LLM Wiki]] — 用 LLM 自动构建和维护 Markdown 个人知识库的模式
52	
53	## 实体（entities/）
54	
55	### 模型/架构
56	- [[wiki/entities/Transformer]] — 2017 年革命性的注意力驱动序列建模架构
57	- [[wiki/entities/BERT]] — Google 2018 年提出的 Encoder-Only 双向预训练模型，革新 NLP 理解任务
58	- [[wiki/entities/GPT]] — OpenAI 的 Decoder-Only 自回归语言模型系列，验证了规模法则
59	- [[wiki/entities/T5]] — Google 2019 年提出的 Encoder-Decoder 模型，统一所有 NLP 任务为 Text-to-Text 生成
60	
61	### 人物
62	- [[wiki/entities/Ashish Vaswani]] — Transformer 论文第一作者，提出"只用 Attention，不用递归"的革命性架构
63	- [[wiki/entities/Dzmitry Bahdanau]] — 注意力机制先驱，2014 年提出 Bahdanau Attention，打破 Seq2Seq 信息瓶颈
64	- [[wiki/entities/Jay Alammar]] — 技术教育家，The Illustrated Transformer 等可视化教程作者
65	- [[wiki/entities/Andrej Karpathy]] — AI 研究员、OpenAI 联合创始人、LLM Wiki 提出者
66	
67	### 工具
68	- [[wiki/entities/Obsidian]] — 本地优先的 Markdown 笔记应用，LLM Wiki 的前端界面

77	- [[wiki/comparisons/positional-encoding-comparison|位置编码方案对比]] — 正弦编码 / 可学习编码 / RoPE / ALiBi 四种方案的优劣与选型指南
78	- [[wiki/comparisons/layernorm-vs-batchnorm|LayerNorm vs BatchNorm]] — 两种归一化方法的核心差异与 Transformer 选择 LayerNorm 的原因
79	- [[wiki/comparisons/encoder-only-vs-decoder-only-vs-encoder-decoder|Encoder-Only vs Decoder-Only vs Encoder-Decoder]] — 三种 Transformer 架构变体的设计哲学、核心差异与选型指南
80	- [[wiki/comparisons/dense-vs-moe|Dense vs MoE]] — Dense 模型与混合专家架构在激活方式、计算量和适用场景上的对比

86	- [[wiki/overviews/modern-llm-landscape|现代 LLM 格局概述]] — 三条技术路线、效率优化技术、2017-2026 发展脉络与架构选型指南

177	- [[raw/lessons/LangChain/06-memory-state-history-boundaries|第 6 章：Memory / State / History——哪些属于组件层，哪些属于系统层]] — 讲清 messages history、conversation state 与 cross-conversation store 的边界，明确 short-term memory 更接近 state，long-term memory 更接近 store，以及哪些职责属于 LangChain 组件层、哪些仍要交给 harness 决策

180	
181	- [[raw/lessons/Agent-Engineering/01-claude-code-like-systems-overview|第 1 章：Claude Code-like 系统总览]] — 从公开能力面拆解 coding agent 的最小模块，明确 MVP / intermediate / advanced 三层工程路线
182	- [[raw/lessons/Agent-Engineering/02-harness-mvp-architecture|第 2 章：Harness MVP 架构——一个最小 coding agent 系统怎么拆]] — 讲清最小 coding agent 的骨架：交互、session、planner、executor、tools、permission、review、logging、config 为什么缺一不可
183	- [[raw/lessons/Agent-Engineering/03-planner-executor-reviewer-loop|第 3 章：Planner / Executor / Reviewer——受控执行回路是怎样形成的]] — 讲清任务如何在规划、执行、复查之间形成可纠偏的主回路，并说明 permission gate 在其中的闸门位置
184	- [[raw/lessons/Agent-Engineering/04-tool-layer-read-search-edit-run|第 4 章：工具层——读文件、搜代码、编辑文件、运行命令为什么构成最小 action surface]] — 把 `read / search / edit / run` 解释为 coding agent 的最小动作面，并说明为什么工具层是系统对外动作的法定出口
185	- [[raw/lessons/Agent-Engineering/05-permissions-and-confirmation-boundaries|第 5 章：权限与确认边界——为什么高风险动作不能直接放行]] — 讲清 allow / ask / deny 三类决策、确认机制的中断语义，以及权限边界如何决定 agent 的行动权
186	- [[raw/lessons/Agent-Engineering/06-session-memory-checkpoint-resume|第 6 章：Session、Memory、Checkpoint、Resume——为什么不能把状态全塞进一个记忆盒子]] — 区分会话状态、长期记忆、可恢复快照与恢复流程，避免把所有状态混成一个大容器
187	- [[raw/lessons/Agent-Engineering/07-task-decomposition-and-multi-step-strategy|第 7 章：任务分解与多步执行策略——复杂任务为什么不能只靠临场乱试]] — 讲清任务形状判断、顺序 / 分层 / 条件分支拆解、局部上下文打包，以及 retry / 局部补救 / replan 怎样在真实 harness 中协同发生
188	- [[raw/lessons/Agent-Engineering/08-project-understanding-and-repo-exploration|第 8 章：项目理解与仓库探索——为什么 agent 不能一上来就盲改代码]] — 讲清目录结构扫描、模式发现、局部精读三层仓库探索策略，以及探索结果如何被压缩成后续步骤可复用的项目上下文对象

- 创建了 [[raw/lessons/CortexDebate/06-conclusion|06-conclusion]] 结论与局限性翻译，包含论文结论、两大局限（效率/成本 + 受限于底层 LLM 能力）、初始答案生成与答案再生成 Prompt 设计、7 种基线方法详细介绍、致谢、整体评价（4 亮点 + 4 不足）

## 论文信息
- PDF 路径：D:\note\raw\assets\papers\MARCH_2026.pdf
- 论文主题：MARCH — 多智能体强化学习自检（2026 最新）

## 任务要求
请阅读论文 PDF，然后创建以下文件（所有文件使用 UTF-8 编码）：

### 1. 00-essence.md — 精华总结
格式参考：
```yaml
---
type: lesson
tags: [MARCH, 多智能体强化学习, 自检机制, 幻觉检测, 论文笔记]

  - MARCH_2026.pdf
difficulty: advanced
prerequisites:
  - "[[wiki/concepts/multi-agent-system]]"
topic: MARCH 论文精华总结
status: completed
---
```

必须包含以下 section（使用二级标题）：
- **论文元信息**：标题、作者、机构、会议/期刊、发表时间、arXiv ID（如有）、代码链接（如有），用表格
- **核心问题**：论文要解决什么问题，1-3句话
- **核心方法**：方法的技术要点，包括关键公式和算法思路
- **实验设置**：数据集、基线、指标、基础设施
- **主要实验结果**：用表格展示关键数据对比
- **论文不足与可改进之处**：至少列出3-5点
- **导航**：链接到所有分篇翻译笔记

### 2. 分篇翻译笔记
根据论文实际结构拆分，一般包括：
- 01-introduction.md — 引言
- 02-related-work.md — 相关工作
- 03-framework.md — 框架（包含三智能体流水线和信息不对称设计）
- 04-information-asymmetry.md — 信息不对称机制（如论文有独立章节）
- 05-experiments.md — 实验
- 06-conclusion.md — 结论（可合并到实验）

每篇翻译笔记格式：
```yaml
---
type: lesson
tags: [MARCH, 翻译笔记, ...其他相关标签]
created: 2026-05-16
updated: 2026-05-16
sources:
  - MARCH_2026.pdf
difficulty: advanced
prerequisites:
  - "[[raw/lessons/MARCH/00-essence]]"
topic: MARCH — [章节名]
status: completed
---
```

### 翻译质量要求（极其重要）
1. **基于原文精准翻译**，不得大幅意译或改写
2. **原文翻译**直接放在 `## 正文翻译` 下的各级标题中，不加额外 callout
3. **非原文补充**必须用 callout 严格区分：
   - 译者注：`> [!note] 译者注（非原文）`
   - 理解提示：`> [!tip] 理解提示（非原文）`
   - 易混点：`> [!warning] 易混点（非原文）`
4. 非原文补充应精简，核心内容必须来自原论文
5. 保留原文的公式、表格、图表描述
6. 每篇 3000-5000 字正文，信息密度要高
7. 中文翻译，技术术语保留英文原文（如 "Information Asymmetry"、"MARL"、"Solver-Proposer-Checker" 等）

### 阅读指南中的关键信息
- 核心思路：三智能体流水线（Solver → Proposer → Checker），关键创新是信息不对称——Checker 只看到分解后的原子命题和证据，看不到 Solver 原始输出，打破自确认偏误
- 亮点：用 MARL 让三个智能体共同进化，8B 参数模型达到闭源模型水平
- 阅读重点：§3 Framework + §3.3 Information Asymmetry + §4 Experiments

MARCH: Multi-Agent Reinforced Self-Check for LLM Hallucination

Qwen Large Model Application Team, Alibaba
Abstract
Hallucination remains a critical bottleneck for large language models (LLMs), undermining their reliability in real-world applications, especially in Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems.
While existing hallucination detection methods employ LLM-as-a-judge to verify LLM outputs against retrieved evidence, they suffer from inherent
confirmation bias
, where the verifier inadvertently reproduces the errors of the original generation.
To address this, we introduce
M
ulti-
A
gent
R
einforced self-
C
heck for
H
allucination (MARCH), a framework that enforces rigorous factual alignment by leveraging deliberate
information asymmetry
.
MARCH orchestrates a collaborative pipeline of three specialized agents: a Solver, a Proposer, and a Checker.
The Solver generates an initial RAG response, which the Proposer decomposes into claim-level verifiable atomic propositions. Crucially, the Checker validates these propositions against retrieved evidence in isolation, deprived of the Solver’s original output.
This well-crafted information asymmetry scheme breaks the cycle of self-confirmation bias. By training this pipeline with multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL), we enable the agents to co-evolve and optimize factual adherence. Extensive experiments across hallucination benchmarks demonstrate that MARCH substantially reduces hallucination rates. Notably, an 8B-parameter LLM equipped with MARCH achieves performance competitive with powerful closed-source models. MARCH paves a scalable path for factual self-improvement of LLMs through co-evolution. The code is at
https://github.com/Qwen-Applications/MARCH
.
Figure 1
:
Overview of the MARCH framework. The
Solver
conducts retrieval-augmented generation base on the input query and related documents. The
Proposer
then decomposes this response into atomic propositions (the orange line in the response block) and formulates verifiable question-answer pairs. The
Checker
performs isolated verification by re-answering questions solely based on the retrieved evidence, without access to the
Solver
’s original output. This information-asymmetric pipeline, where the policy model plays all three roles, is optimized via Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) to achieve robust factual alignment.
1
Introduction
Despite the remarkable success achieved by Large Language Models (LLMs)
(

;
touvron2023llamaopenefficientfoundation
;
yang2024qwen2
;
deepseekai2025deepseekr1incentivizingreasoningcapability
;
comanici2025gemini25pushingfrontier
)
,
the persistence of
hallucinations
remains a fundamental barrier to LLMs’ trustworthy usage
(
kalai2025language
;
Huang_2025
;
ji2023survey
;
maynez2020faithfulnessfactualityabstractivesummarization
)
. Even enhanced within Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
(
lewis2020retrieval
)
, LLMs frequently generate responses that, while linguistically fluent, contradict the provided evidentiary context
(
xu2025hallucinationinevitableinnatelimitation
)
. Such context-conflicting hallucinations
(
liu2024lost
)
are particularly prevalent in data-intensive tasks, manifesting as inaccurate numbers, distorted temporal sequences, or erroneous arithmetic aggregations, even when the source text explicitly contains the requisite information. In high-stakes domains, such as finance
(
kang2023deficiency
)
, law
(
dahl2024large
)
, and healthcare
(
kim2025medical
)
,
hallucinations are more prohibitive, as users require LLMs to function as meticulous evidence readers rather than creative generators of plausible but unverified narratives.
Among approaches in mitigating hallucinations, Supervised Fine-Tune (SFT) has been primarily considered as effective
(
elaraby2023haloestimationreductionhallucinations
;
lin2022truthfulqameasuringmodelsmimic
;
zhou2023limaalignment
)
. However, several practices emphasize that SFT could also amplify hallucinations by fundamentally operating on behavior cloning
(
torabi2018behavioral
)
, which induces models to prioritize authoritative stylistic patterns over factual accuracy
(
casper2023openproblemsfundamentallimitations
;
zhang2025sirenssongaiocean
;
gekhman2024doesfinetuningllmsnew
)
.
Consequently, Reinforcement Learning (RL) has become a necessity for optimizing LLMs towards more truthfulness
(
yang2025qwen3technicalreport
;
gemmateam2025gemma3technicalreport
)
.
However, the standard outcome-level RL rewards lack the granularity required to supervise fine-grained factual consistency, leading to a fundamental misalignment between the scalar reward and the nuanced evidentiary requirements of RAG.
Specifically, Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF)
(
ouyang2022training
)
typically relies on reward models (RMs)
(
bradley1952rank
;
li2025eliminatinginductivebiasreward
;
li-etal-2025-aplot
;
liu2024skyworkrewardbagtricksreward
)
that compress multifaceted evaluations into coarse-grained scalar scores. This loss of granularity limits the ability to verify individual claims, providing insufficient signals to reinforce the meticulous evidentiary grounding required for data-intensive tasks. Alternatively, while Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Reward (RLVR)
(
deepseekai2025deepseekr1incentivizingreasoningcapability
)
attempts to provide objective feedback via deterministic rules
(
shao2024deepseekmathpushinglimitsmathematical
)
, rubrics
(
gunjal2025rubricsrewardsreinforcementlearning
)
, or external generalist reward model
(
liu2025inferencetimescalinggeneralistreward
)
, it remains fundamentally constrained by a dual-dependency bottleneck: the scarcity of expert-annotated groundtruth in specialized domains, and the reasoning ceilings of external verifiers. Consequently, unsupported intermediate claims can persist, as models are rewarded for correct final outcomes despite potentially hallucinated reasoning trajectories
(
li2025reasoningmodelshallucinatemore
)
.
While post-hoc metrics and fact-checkers
(
tang2024minicheck
;
rashkin2023measuring
;
gao-etal-2023-rarr
;
baker2025monitoringreasoningmodelsmisbehavior
;
guan2025monitoringmonitorability
)
provide utility for evaluation, such static tools are seldom integrated into the internal optimization loop to provide actionable, claim-oriented guidance. More critically, the efficacy of such verifiers is fundamentally undermined by the
information leakage
inherent in current evaluation schemes
(
seo2025verifyingverifiersunveilingpitfalls
)
.
Specifically, concurrent exposure to the query, documents, and generated response induces a confirmation bias
(
sharma2025understandingsycophancylanguagemodels
)
. In this state, verifiers tend to prioritize validating internal coherence over objective grounding against source evidence, leading to the unwarranted endorsement of erroneous claims. This compromised “blind scrutiny” prevents the model from internalizing the rigorous, document-anchored reasoning essential for high-fidelity generation.
To address above factuality challenges, we introduce
M
ulti-
A
gent
R
einforced self-
C
heck for
H
allucination (MARCH), an RL-based framework that enforces factual alignment through deliberate information asymmetry.
MARCH orchestrates a collaborative pipeline of three specialized agents: a
Solver
, a
Proposer
, and a
Checker
, to prevent model-based verifiers from taking cognitive shortcuts through confirmation bias.
As illustrated in Figure
1
, the Solver first generates a response based on the query and retrieved documents. Subsequently, the Proposer acts as a ’Response Atomizer,’ deconstructing the narrative into discrete, verifiable claims. Finally, the Checker validates these claims by re-answering them exclusively based on the source documents, strictly blinded to the Solver’s original output to avoid cognitive contamination. Through this asymmetric decoupling, MARCH transforms the verification process from a circular confirmation of plausible text into a rigorous cross-examination between generated claims and factual evidence.
During training, MARCH promotes agentic self-evolution via a zero-tolerance reward mechanism: any discrepancy between the Proposer’s extracted claims and the Checker’s document-based validation triggers a penalty on the entire response trajectory. This encourages the agents to align their outputs strictly with retrieved evidence, effectively internalizing the capability for rigorous, document-anchored statements.
Experimentally, MARCH achieves a substantial reduction in hallucinations compared to its base model, without requiring additional human annotations or external fact-checking tools, thereby demonstrating the efficacy of our asymmetric collaborative reinforcement paradigm. Notably, an 8B-parameter LLM equipped with MARCH achieves performance parity with leading closed-source models on multiple hallucination benchmarks, while simultaneously delivering significant gains in general RAG-QA tasks. These results establish a scalable and verifiable pathway toward trustworthy, agentic self-improvement for large language models.

Algorithm 1
MARCH Training Process.
3
Experiment
We first evaluate the effectiveness of MARCH in mitigating hallucinations.
Then, we show that the improved factual consistency ability helps downstream reasoning tasks like Question-Answering.
3.1
Training Setup
Training Datasets and Base Model.
We train our framework covering: a STEM (Science-Technology-Engineering-Math) setting based on BioASQ
(
krithara2023bioasq
)
, and a General setting based on 2WikiMultiHopQA
(
ho2020constructing
)
and MuSiQue
(
trivedi2022musique
)
. Crucially, training is performed using only queries and retrieved documents, without access to ground-truth answers or annotations. Table
1
shows the number of retrieved documents per query and the document lengths vary widely across datasets, and a substantial fraction of the documents are irrelevant to the query (e.g., 30.1% for STEM and up to 88.4% for MuSiQue). Such a high level of noise and heterogeneity in the retrieval docs makes the training setting highly realistic but also extremely challenging, as the agents must learn to perform document-grounded fact checking in the presence of many noisy documents. We consider Meta-Llama3.1-8B-Instruct
(
touvron2023llamaopenefficientfoundation
)
as the initialization policy.
Table 1
:
Statistics of the training dataset, where we report the number of training samples, average query length, the number of retrieved documents per query, the average document length, and the proportion of documents that are irrelevant to the query. The large variance in document counts and lengths, together with the high ratio of irrelevant documents, highlights the realism and difficulty of our training setup.

Training Details.
Training framework is built upon the
VerL
(
Sheng_2025
)
, and we employ Fully Sharded Data Parallel (FSDP)
(
zhao2023pytorchfsdpexperiencesscaling
)
for efficient multi-node multi-GPU training. During the training rollout, we utilize the
vLLM
(
kwon2023efficientmemorymanagementlarge
)
for efficient sample generation with the temperature of 0.6. The PPO
(
schulman2017proximalpolicyoptimizationalgorithms
)
training is conducted for a single epoch with a global batch size of 32, with a max prompt length of 24,567, max response length of 8,192. We set learning rates for the actor and the critic as
1
×
10
−
6
1\times 10^{-6}
and
1
×
10
−
5
1\times 10^{-5}
, respectively. We save and evaluate a checkpoint every 20 steps, and report results from the single best-performing checkpoint selected based on the highest average score across all evaluation benchmarks. More detailed training hyper-parameters are in Appendix
B.1
.
3.2
Experiments on Hallucination
Evaluation Benchmark.
We evaluate MARCH along the aspect of
hallucination and factual grounding
on the RAGTruth
(
niu2024ragtruth
)
, FaithBench
(
tamber2025benchmarking
)
, ContextualJudgeBench
(
xu2025does
)
and Facts Grounding benchmark
(
jacovi2025facts
)
, which cover QA, data-to-text writing, summarization, and diverse domains such as medical, financial, and legal. Detailed introductions are provided in Appendix
B.2.1
. The best performance is marked with
Red
and the second-best with
Blue
.
Evaluation Protocol and Metrics.
Following
niu2024ragtruth
;
tamber2025benchmarking
;
jacovi2025facts
;
xu2025does
, we focus on
response-level
factuality eval. Given generation, retrieved documents, and ground-truth reference, we deploy a Qwen3-235B-A22B
(
yang2025qwen3technicalreport
)
as a judge model to decide whether the response is factually consistent/accurate with the evidence/ground-truth factual labels. We mitigate the randomness during evaluation by drawing eight independent generations for each test query; the judge model then evaluates each sampling individually, and the final verdict is determined by a majority vote across these eight trajectories.
Results on RAGTruth and FaithBench.
Table
2
shows that our method brings substantial and consistent improvements over the base Llama3.1-8B-Instruct across all settings. With STEM data (
MARCH-STEM
), the learned policy improves the average accuracy from 55.20 to 74.93 (+19.73) and with General QA data (
MARCH-General
), the average accuracy further rises to 75.23 (+20.03), which suggest that MARCH reliably strengthens the base model’s ability to judge and mitigate hallucinations, rather than overfitting to a particular domain. When compared to other larger or proprietary LLMs, our policies also achieve an advanced average performance. Together, these results demonstrate that our framework can turn a small-sized base LLM into a highly competitive factuality judge simply by training on noisy, document-grounded RAG data.

Results on Facts Grounding.
FACTS Grounding evaluates whether model responses are fully supported by a single source document. Figure
2
shows that our policies achieve large gains over the base model, reaching a factuality score of 85.23% (MARCH-STEM) and 80.12% (MARCH-General), compared with 57.09% for the base Llama3.1‑8B-Instruct. Our method successfully lifts an 8B backbone into the comparable performance band as leading proprietary models, placing
MARCH‑STEM
just behind the top Gemini 2.5/1.5 Flash models on the public leaderboard while surpassing popular models such as GPT‑4o and several larger open models. The strong results indicate that MARCH substantially improves long‑document grounding robustness across heterogeneous domains.

:

Results on ContextualJudgeBench.
Table
3
reports results on ContextualJudgeBench, which evaluates multiple dimensions of judge behavior, including refusal, faithfulness, completeness, and conciseness. Starting from Llama3.1-8B, our framework again yields large gains across all eight dimensions under both training regimes. Specifically, the policy trained on General QA data (
MARCH-General
) improves the average accuracy from 29.7 to 51.6 (+21.9), while the policy trained on STEM data (
MARCH-STEM
) reaches 52.3 (+22.6). The improvements are particularly pronounced on faithfulness and completeness (both QA and summarization), where accurate document-grounded judgment is most critical. Based on an 8B LLM, our policies surprisingly reach or surpass the performance of many much larger judge models, and approach the best specialized judge models on several dimensions. Table
3
indicates that MARCH not only improves binary hallucination detection but also equips LLM with a more nuanced ability to assess answer quality along multiple axes, such as whether key information is covered and whether responses remain concise and well-calibrated in context.
3.3
Experiments on Multi-Hop QA
Intuitively, the capacity for precise factual extraction and verification reasonably serves as the fundamental bedrock for complex reasoning, as Question Answering tasks usually require a model to sequentially bridge information across disparate documents.
In this section, we empirically find that the improved factual grounding brought by MARCH can directly translate into better performance on reasoning-intensive downstream tasks.
Evaluation Benchmarks.
Following
xu2025collab
;
trivedi2023interleaving
;
shao2023enhancing
, we evaluate the performance of our MARCH on three popular multi-hop QA datasets, including HotpotQA
(
yang2018hotpotqa
)
, 2WikiMultiHopQA
(
ho2020constructing
)
and MuSiQue
(
trivedi2022musique
)
. More details about datasets are provided in Appendix
B.2.2
.
Evaluation Protocol and Metrics.
Following
xu2025collab
;
shao2023enhancing
;
trivedi2023interleaving
, we sample the first 500 examples from the development set of each dataset as ou

Comparison with Existing Baselines.
Table
4
compares MARCH-Best, which incorporates joint optimization of both the Solver and the Checker alongside CoT prompting, with various specialized RAG methods and proprietary models. Within the 8B-parameter scale, MARCH achieves higher accuracy on the HotpotQA and MuSiQue benchmarks compared to methods such as RAFT
(
dong2023raftrewardrankedfinetuning
)
and GenGround
(
shi2024generate
)
. Notably, on the HotpotQA benchmark, which contains a high density of noisy documents, MARCH reaches an accuracy of 71.2%, exceeding the reported results of standard GPT-4o RAG (64.0%) and the IRCoT
(
trivedi2023interleaving
)
framework using GPT-4o (66.4%).
Table
4
suggests that MARCH can effectively enhance the factual reliability of smaller models in multi-hop reasoning scenarios, leading to performance that is comparable to and even higher than much larger proprietary models.
3.4
Ablation Study and Further Investigation
Performance Analysis of Joint Optimization.
Table
5
illustrates that the joint optimization of the Solver and the Checker consistently achieves the highest accuracy across all training strategies in both STEM and General benchmarks. A key observation is that while the Solver-only update provides a base improvement, the inclusion of the Checker significantly amplifies the model’s reasoning capabilities, particularly in complex scenarios. Specifically, in the
Few-Shot
setting for STEM, the joint model achieves a remarkable absolute gain of 11.6% over the Solver-only version. These results suggest that the Checker’s auditing signal is crucial for leveraging the full potential of advanced prompting techniques like
few-shot
and
CoT
, as it effectively mitigates factual drift and ensures logical consistency throughout multi-hop reasoning trajectories. In summary, Table
5
suggests that by internalizing a claim-level auditing logic, MARCH significantly amplifies the efficacy of in-context learning and multi-step reasoning, ensuring that increased model capacity translates into verifiable factual accuracy rather than hallucinated complexity.
Does our method conflict with other boosting methods?
As demonstrated in Table
5
, MARCH is highly orthogonal and can be seamlessly integrated with existing performance-boosting techniques, including RLHF, Few-Shot prompting, and CoT reasoning. When applied to the Llama3.1-8B-Instruct baseline, our method consistently achieves significant performance gains across all benchmarks. For instance, combining MARCH with CoT reasoning further elevates the average accuracy on STEM tasks from 50.93% to 59.13%, and on General tasks from 51.00% to 57.80%. Similarly, notable improvements are observed when MARCH is integrated with Few-Shot (10-Shots) configurations, reaching an average of 61.27% on STEM. Table
5
indicates that MARCH does not conflict with traditional scaling or prompting methods; rather, it provides a complementary layer of factual rigor through its asymmetric self-play mechanism, leading to a cumulative enhancement in multi-hop question-answering capabilities. We provide the implementation details in Appendix
B.3
.

We evaluate two reward formulations: (i) Error Rate Reward (ERR), a proportional penalty based on the ratio of incorrect claims, which is defined as

Performance on Different Model Family.
As shown in the last group in Table
6
, the MARCH framework exhibits robust generalizability across different model families, with Qwen3-8B reflecting the same performance trajectory observed in Llama-based experiments. Specifically, MARCH elevates Qwen3-8B to average accuracies of 67.90% and 68.11% under General and STEM settings, respectively, marking a significant absolute gain of over 11% compared to its vanilla baseline (56.84%) and demonstrating that the dual-trajectory optimization mechanism is model-agnostic and consistently internalizes superior evidentiary grounding across diverse architectures.

Additional Analysis
We analyze the potential reward hacking in MARCH and provide a primary solution (Appendix
B.4
). We visualize the PPO training dynamics and analyze the time consumption (Appendix
B.5

4
Related Work
Hallucinations and Fact-Check in RAG.
While RAG reduces fabrications by anchoring responses in external context
(
lewis2020retrieval
)
, LLMs remain susceptible to context-conflicting hallucinations
(
shuster2021retrievalaugmentationreduceshallucination
)
. Current mitigation efforts largely revolve around outcome-level supervision
(
shi2023replugretrievalaugmentedblackboxlanguage
;
gekhman2024doesfinetuningllmsnew
;
yu2024chainofnoteenhancingrobustnessretrievalaugmented
;
asai2023selfraglearningretrievegenerate
)
, post-hoc reasoning refinements and utilizing external verifiers to filter outputs during inference
(
dhuliawala2023chainofverificationreduceshallucinationlarge
;
madaan2023selfrefineiterativerefinementselffeedback
;
min-etal-2023-factscore
;
rashkin2023measuring
;
gao-etal-2023-rarr
;
tang2024minicheck
)
. However, these methods often treat LLM as a black box and fail to internalize factual constraints within the training objective itself. Moving beyond, we propose a framework where the model learns to prioritize factual adherence by minimizing logical discrepancies identified through an internal audit.
Multi-Agent Systems for LLMs.
Multi-agent systems have emerged as a robust paradigm for enhancing LLM performance through collaborative or adversarial interactions
(
du2023improvingfactualityreasoninglanguage
;
li2023camel
)
, which typically assign distinct personas to one / multiple model instances to facilitate code generation
(
huang2023agentcoder
;
islam2024mapcoder
)
, complex reasoning
(
li2023camel
;
he2023lego
;
talebirad2023multi
)
, decision-making
(
shinn2023reflexion
;
yu2024fincon
)
, recommendation
(
wang2024macrec
)
, and debate-driven consensus
(
du2023improvingfactualityreasoninglanguage
;
liang2024encouraging
)
. While effective at inference time, most existing architectures treat agents as separate entities, often overlooking the potential for joint policy optimization across different functional roles. In addition, previous work shows that multi-agent system sometimes amplifies the hallucination of LLMs
(
hong2023metagpt
)
. Our framework differs by instantiating functionally distinct roles within a single shared policy. By structurally enforcing an information barrier between agents, we facilitate the co-evolution of generative and evaluative capabilities within a unified training loop, ensu

5
Conclusion
In this paper, we introduced MARCH, a multi-agent framework designed to mitigate hallucinations in data-intensive RAG tasks. By reconfiguring a shared policy into a coordinated hierarchy comprising a Solver, a Proposer, and a Checker, MARCH enables LLMs to simultaneously refine their synthesis reasoning and independent grounding capabilities. Our experiments demonstrate that the combination of a Zero-Tolerance Reward mechanism and dual-trajectory PPO effectively internalizes a self-contained verification loop, consistently outperforming strong baselines. The robust generalization of MARCH across diverse model families underscores its potential as a scalable, high-fidelity solution for information synthesis in complex knowledge domains. We believe this agentic co-evolution paradigm offers a promising pathway toward building inherently trustworthy and verifiable large language models.
